Yes, mutations in the COL1A2 and COL1A1 genes of type I collagen cause autosomal dominant osteogenesis imperfecta. Additionally, the more prevalent autosomal dominant forms of the disorder are caused by primary defects in type I collagen.
Yes, apremilast is effective for the management of oral and genital ulcers associated with Behçet’s syndrome, and it is generally well tolerated, as shown in a randomized controlled trial.

Yes, splicing speckles are associated with transcription. While they don’t act as major storage sites for inactive RNA polymerase II, they contain a stable pool of polymerase II phosphorylated on serine residues, which is transcriptionally inactive and may be involved in spliceosome assembly or posttranscriptional splicing. Additionally, activation of transgene transcription results in a closer positioning of pIndi bodies to splicing speckles, and progression of NHPX from speckles to nucleoli is dependent on RNA polymerase II transcription.

Miravirsen is an anti-miR-122 oligonucleotide that sequesters miR-122, thereby inhibiting hepatitis C virus replication. It has been successfully tested in clinical Phase II trials for the treatment of hepatitis C and functions by suppressing HCV replication after infection and preventing infection with pretreatment. Delivering miravirsen during normothermic ex vivo liver perfusion is a potential strategy to prevent HCV reinfection after liver transplantation.

Centor criteria are used for diagnosing Group A-beta Hemolytic Streptococcus (GABHS) tonsillopharyngitis or for identifying patients with a higher likelihood of group A streptococcal infection causing acute sore throat.

No. Studies show that enzastaurin did not significantly improve overall survival compared to lomustine, and a meta-analysis indicated no improvement in overall survival with antiangiogenic drugs including enzastaurin. While one study showed a median overall survival of 15.0 months with enzastaurin and radiation in patients without MGMT promoter hypermethylation, another showed only 6.6 months with enzastaurin compared to 7.1 months with lomustine in recurrent glioblastoma. Additionally, a phase III study was terminated early due to futility.

Yes, tofacitinib has demonstrated efficacy in treating ulcerative colitis. It is an oral Janus kinase inhibitor that has shown potential as both induction and maintenance therapy, inducing and maintaining clinical remission in trials involving patients with moderately to severely active ulcerative colitis, even those previously treated with other therapies. It has recently been approved for the treatment of moderate-to-severe ulcerative colitis.
The main transcriptional activators of circadian oscillations are CLOCK and BMAL1, with Bmal1 being essential, and these are further activated by CRY1, CRY2, and PER2.

Yes, CREB appears to be a key memory protein. It regulates development, protection, learning, memory, and plasticity in the nerve system, and is implicated in both long-term and short-term memory formation in various organisms, including mice, nematodes, and rats. Its activation via phosphorylation is essential for memory processes, and deficiencies in CREB homologs result in defective memory. Furthermore, CREB-mediated gene expression is required for the consolidation of social recognition memory.

Tofacitinib was investigated in the Oral treatment for Psoriasis Trial Pivotal 1 and 2, OPT Pivotal 1 and 2, the Oral-treatment Psoriasis Trial Compare Study, and a long-term extension study following the Pivotal trials.
Ustekinumab inhibits interleukins 12 and 23, as it is a monoclonal antibody directed against the p40 subunit shared by both of these interleukins.

Abaloparatide, also known as Tymlos, is an osteoanabolic agent developed for the treatment of postmenopausal osteoporosis and acts through selective activation of the parathyroid hormone type 1 receptor signalling pathway.
ALK-positive non-small-cell lung cancer has been approved for treatment with ALK inhibitors such as crizotinib, ceritinib, alectinib, and brigatinib.

Cytotoxic T-lymphocyte-associated protein-4, programmed cell death protein-1, T-cell immunoglobulin and ITIM domain, T-cell immunoglobulin-3, lymphocyte activation gene 3, and PD-L1 are targeted by immune checkpoint inhibitors.

The Aux/IAA family includes 29 genes in Arabidopsis, Sl-IAA genes in tomato, at least 15 expressed sequence tags in wheat including TaIAA1, and IAA5 and IAA19 genes, all of which encode short-lived nuclear proteins with four conserved domains—I, II, III, and IV—that function as active repressors of auxin-dependent gene transcription.

GenomeVIP is an open-source framework for genomic variant discovery and annotation. It utilizes cloud- or local high-performance computing infrastructure and orchestrates analysis of genomic data using tools like VarScan, GATK, and others through a web interface. GenomeVIP has been applied to large-data projects including TCGA PanCanAtlas and the 1000 Genomes SV Project, and is capable of providing high-confidence annotated variants.

There are eight described types of conjoined twins—cephalopagus, dicephalus (now parapagus), ishiopagus, omphalopagus, pygopagus, thoracopagus, heteropagus, and craniopagi—with the possibility of additional types arising from fusion at the pharyngeal or cloacal membranes.
MARS is a new heuristic method that has been developed to improve multiple circular sequence alignment using refined sequences.
A double-click stapled peptide encoding the Ctf4-interacting peptide (CIP) of the replicative helicase subunit Sld5 has been designed to target Ctf4.
Mutation of the SLC2A10 gene causes arterial tortuosity syndrome.
Fusarium oxysporum f. sp. lycopersici is a plant pathogen in tomato plants.

No, while cilengitide demonstrates antiangiogenic and anti-invasive activity in preclinical studies and shows encouraging activity in some trials, an overall survival benefit has not been demonstrated in phase III trials for newly diagnosed or recurrent glioblastoma. Some studies suggest it may be beneficial in combination with other treatments, particularly in tumors with methylated MGMT promoter, and it does deliver to tumor sites, but definitive evidence of effectiveness remains elusive.

BBCAnalyzer (Bases By CIGAR Analyzer) is a visual approach designed to facilitate variant calling, particularly when classic software fails to identify expected mutations. It visualizes base counts at specific positions within sequence alignment data, presenting information like base qualities and known mutations in customizable plots. This tool allows users to evaluate mutations independently of internal filters or frequency thresholds, aiding in the manual inspection of common mutation sites.

Yes, between 5 to 10 percent of patients with multiple myeloma are affected by hyperviscosity syndrome due to increased serum viscosity caused by myeloma protein, and it can lead to complications like bleeding, retinopathy, visual disturbance, and neurological disorders. Several instances detail cases where multiple myeloma is directly linked to the development of this syndrome.

The FaceBase Consortium aims to accelerate understanding of craniofacial developmental biology and ultimately improve healthcare for patients. It achieves this by generating comprehensive data resources, fostering scientific collaborations, and facilitating hypothesis-driven research. A key goal is to create a central resource for craniofacial researchers, including molecular anatomy information and an ontology to integrate diverse data types, ultimately promoting discovery into the causes of craniofacial abnormalities.

TCGA2BED is a software tool used to search, retrieve, and convert TCGA data into the structured BED format. It also supports conversion into other standard formats like CSV, GTF, JSON, and XML. Additionally, TCGA2BED enhances TCGA data by integrating information from other genomic databases, and provides a regularly updated repository of TCGA experimental data in BED format along with associated clinical data. This availability of TCGA data in BED format reduces the time needed to effectively utilize it.

No, LINC-PINT acts as a tumor suppressor by reducing the invasive phenotype of cancer cells and its downregulation is observed in multiple types of cancer. Additionally, a peptide encoded by its circular form suppresses glioblastoma cell proliferation, and low levels correlate with poor prognosis in pancreatic cancer, suggesting it inhibits tumor development.

No, LDB1-mediated enhancer looping can occur independent of cohesin. Studies show that tethering mutant LDB1 to a promoter forced loop formation without cohesin occupancy, and data indicates cohesin is almost completely absent from many LDB1-regulated enhancer-gene pairs. Additionally, LDB1-mediated looping was restored without mediator core occupancy, further suggesting independence from other factors often associated with looping.

Nursemaid’s elbow, also known as a pulled elbow or subluxated radial head, is a common injury among children, most frequently occurring in those aged 1 to 4 years, and sometimes infants as young as six months. It typically results from axial traction applied to an extended, pronated arm or from activities like yanking an outstretched arm to prevent a fall. While often easily treated and without long-term effects, atypical cases may require imaging like MRI to diagnose entrapment of the annular ligament and confirm successful reduction after manipulation. The injury can often be diagnosed clinically, even without radiographic findings.

Trismus is a severely restricted mouth opening of any cause, and a mouth opening of 35 mm or less is considered trismus. It’s characterized by a reduced ability to open the mouth, impacting daily activities like chewing, swallowing, and speaking. Radiotherapy in the head and neck area is a frequent cause of trismus in head and neck cancer patients, and mouth opening can decrease by approximately 20% after radiotherapy.

Benralizumab is a monoclonal antibody that binds to the α subunit of the receptor to interleukin-5 (IL-5Rα). By targeting the IL-5 receptor, it depletes eosinophils and exhibits enhanced antibody-dependent cell-mediated cytotoxicity. This action is beneficial in treating eosinophilic asthma, as IL-5 is key for eosinophil growth, differentiation, recruitment, activation, and survival.

Yes, ERCC1-XPF cooperates with cohesin to facilitate the developmental silencing of imprinted genes, and the two factors, along with CTCF and other proteins, co-localize at promoters and control regions of these genes during development.

MBV is a method designed to solve sample mislabeling and detect technical bias in large genomic datasets that combine genotype and sequence data. It quickly identifies issues like cross-sample contamination and PCR amplification bias. MBV is implemented in C++ as part of the QTLtools software package and is freely available for use.

No, no significant between-group difference in cognitive function was observed between those receiving evolocumab and placebo over a median of 19 months. The study demonstrated noninferiority, meaning evolocumab was not worse than placebo, but did not show improvement.

Yes, carpal tunnel syndrome is a common condition and a well-recognized phenomenon following a distal radius fracture, and can present in acute, transient, or delayed forms. It can also occur as a late onset post-operative complication after volar plating.

Yes, Logic Alignment Free (LAF) is presented as a method that successfully classifies bacterial genomes to their corresponding taxonomy, obtaining reliable classification at different taxonomic levels. It does this by using k-mer frequencies to build classification models in the form of logic formulas.

Yes, Marfan syndrome is associated with chordal rupture. Several instances detail chordal rupture occurring in individuals with Marfan syndrome, including a case of perioperative chordal rupture during a modified Bentall’s operation, acute mitral regurgitation due to chordal rupture in an infant, and chordal rupture as a cause of mitral regurgitation in adults requiring mitral valve procedures. Additionally, the generalized connective tissue disorder present in Marfan syndrome may lead to future elongation and rupture of chordae tendineae.

Fremanezumab is a humanized monoclonal antibody that reduces the availability of calcitonin gene-related peptide (CGRP). It selectively inhibits the responsiveness of Aδ neurons, but not C-fiber neurons, which are primary afferent meningeal nociceptors. This peripheral inhibitory action likely contributes to its effectiveness in preventing headache and its selective inhibition of high-threshold neurons.

Trastuzumab-induced cardiotoxicity is often reversible, and improvement in cardiac function can occur even if trastuzumab therapy is continued or withdrawn. Type II cardiac changes associated with trastuzumab are generally reversible and not dose-related. However, recent findings suggest that trastuzumab can sometimes cause long-term or even irreversible cardiotoxicity, particularly in those with cardiac risk factors, and potentially even in patients without pre-existing conditions.

Nimotuzumab is an anti-EGFR monoclonal antibody used in the treatment of diffuse pontine glioma (DIPG), often in combination with radiotherapy and vinorelbine, or temozolomide. It has shown potential to improve response rates or survival time in DIPG, and is generally well-tolerated, though serious adverse events can occur. Studies have explored its use as both a first-line treatment and after standard therapies have failed, with some evidence suggesting a survival benefit and feasibility of prolonged administration.

Annotatr is a Bioconductor package developed to flexibly and quickly summarize and plot annotations of genomic regions. It reports all intersections of regions and annotations, providing a better understanding of the genomic context of the regions and enabling a richer biological interpretation of experiments. The package also includes graphics functions to easily plot data associated with the regions across annotations and is up to 27 times faster than comparable R packages. It is available at http://bioconductor.org/packages/annotatr/ and https://github.com/rcavalcante/annotatr.

Blount's disease is a progressive form of genu varum, causing a varus alignment of the lower extremities, and results from asymmetrical inhibition of the proximal tibial epiphysis. It can be difficult to differentiate from physiological bowlegs, particularly in early infancy, and may sometimes heal spontaneously, with about a one-in-three chance of recovery without treatment in stage 1 cases. While sometimes considered a progressive condition, bow legs associated with the disease appear to improve with age in some family members, suggesting variable penetrance and potentially mild presentations in parents.

Nusinersen is an antisense oligonucleotide that targets intronic splicing silencer N1 (ISS-N1). By targeting ISS-N1, it corrects SMN2 exon 7 splicing, ultimately promoting production of full-length survival motor neuron (SMN) protein from the SMN2 pseudogene. This action increases SMN protein concentrations, addressing the underlying cause of spinal muscular atrophy caused by low levels of SMN protein due to mutations in the SMN1 gene.

King-Kopetzky syndrome is characterized by auditory disability despite clinically normal hearing thresholds, with a primary symptom being difficulty recognizing and understanding speech in noisy backgrounds. Patients commonly experience difficulties with live and electronic speech, particularly in noise and group conversations, and may also exhibit anxiety. Evidence suggests mild impairment of cochlear function and decreased distortion product otoacoustic emission levels are present in some individuals with the syndrome, and psychological factors like somatic anxiety significantly contribute to speech-hearing disability.

The 959 Nematode Genomes initiative is a community wiki and project launched to inspire, promote, and coordinate genomic sequencing across the diversity of the nematode phylum. It aims to sequence 959 nematode genomes, building upon the success of the *Caenorhabditis elegans* genome sequencing project. This initiative tracks sequencing projects—proposed, in progress, or completed—and fosters collaboration among researchers to advance understanding of nematode biology and parasitic diseases.

Genomic Regulatory Blocks are chromosomal regions spanned by highly conserved non-coding elements, often including target genes—typically transcription factors involved in embryonic development—as well as bystander genes, gene deserts, CpG islands, and distinct patterns of histone modification marks associated with developmental genes.
Glecaprevir and pibrentasvir are direct-acting antiviral agents for the treatment of chronic hepatitis C virus infection.
The drug target for Belsomra is the orexin-1 receptor and orexin-2 receptor.

Yes, canagliflozin is associated with the development of euglycemic diabetic ketoacidosis in patients with both type 1 and type 2 diabetes, and physicians should consider it in the differential diagnosis when patients are treated with this medication. Several cases report ketoacidosis occurring without significant hyperglycemia while taking canagliflozin.

CellMaps is an open-source web tool that displays, edits, explores, and analyzes biological networks. It also integrates metadata into these networks and allows for computations and analyses to be remotely executed on high-end servers. The tool is accessible through RESTful web services and can be integrated into any webpage using a JavaScript API. It is available at http://cellmaps.babelomics.org/.

Yes, individuals with Klinefelter’s syndrome appear to have an increased risk of developing breast cancer, potentially approaching three percent. Reports indicate an association, with some data suggesting a higher incidence, though statistical analysis is limited due to the small number of reported cases. Several reports detail instances of breast cancer occurring in patients with Klinefelter’s syndrome.
SignatureEstimation, signeR, and SomaticSignatures are R packages developed for identifying and analyzing mutational signatures in cancer genomes.
According to guidelines, three additional risk factors for metabolic syndrome—along with insulin resistance—include abdominal obesity, dyslipidemia, and hypertension.

Silent Allosteric Modulation of mGluR5 shows therapeutic potential for Alzheimer’s disease by disrupting the interaction between cellular prion protein and mGluR5, which plays a pivotal role in the pathogenesis of the disease.

Check point inhibitors are molecules, including monoclonal antibodies, that empower anti-cancer immune responses by interfering with inhibitory receptors such as CTLA-4, PD-1, and LAG3, and function as tumor suppressing factors via modulation of immune cell-tumor cell interaction.

Yes, both GRPR and NMBR are G protein coupled receptors and members of the bombesin receptor family that are frequently overexpressed by tumors, including lung cancer. One study investigates a bombesin receptor subtype-3 antagonist which inhibits lung cancer growth, and another states that members of the gastrin-releasing peptide family and its analogs bombesin have been implicated in the biology of several human cancers including lung.
Cseq-Simulator is a data simulator available for PAR-CLIP, HITS-CLIP and iCLIP-data that can generate realistic datasets as surrogates for experimental gold standard datasets.
The falciform ligament sign is characteristic of pneumoperitoneum and is more frequent in proximal gastrointestinal perforation.

Yes, vorinostat shows some activity in recurrent glioblastoma and is being investigated in combination with other treatments like bevacizumab, CPT-11, and temozolomide to improve progression-free and overall survival, though early trials have shown only modest benefit and larger trials are needed to confirm its value.

Yes, Dlx1 and Dlx2 transcription factors promote GABA synthesis, synaptogenesis, and dendritogenesis in cortical interneurons, and are involved in the fate switch between cortical and striatal interneurons. They also coordinate key components of cortical interneuron postnatal development by promoting their excitability, inhibitory output, and survival.

SNP2TFBS is a computational resource for researchers studying regulatory variation in the human genome. It’s a collection of text files annotating human single nucleotide polymorphisms (SNPs) and predicting their effect on transcription factor binding site affinity—whether they abolish, create, or change it. This prediction is based on a position weight matrix model, and the data is regularly updated using a reference genome, SNP catalogue, and collection of position weight matrices. Users can access SNP2TFBS through a web interface to view information, search for SNPs, annotate sets of SNPs, or display statistics.

SMiLE-seq is a novel, semiautomated technology used to characterize protein-DNA interactions. It resolves the DNA-binding specificities of transcription factors and probes these properties over a wide affinity range in a fast and cost-effective manner. This technology is not limited by DNA bait length or biased toward strong affinity binders and has been validated by analyzing transcription factors from different structural classes.

libgapmis is a library designed for extending pairwise short-read alignments. It includes CPU, SSE, and GPU-based implementations and utilizes an algorithm computing a modified dynamic-programming matrix for sequence alignment. This library is more efficient than existing algorithms and can be seamlessly integrated into short-read alignment pipelines, offering flexibility to split reads into fragments based on gap occurrence.

Yes, ATF4 has been linked to cancer and neoplastic transformation. It is frequently upregulated in cancer cells and promotes oncogene-induced neoplastic transformation by suppressing cellular senescence, and its expression is elevated in various cancers including endometrial and breast cancer where it contributes to tumor growth and angiogenesis.

Yes, patients with Sjogren syndrome have a heightened or increased risk for developing lymphoma, particularly non-Hodgkin’s lymphoma. Several sources confirm this predisposition, noting that the risk is well established and that the syndrome is known to be associated with lymphoma development. The risk is higher in patients with primary Sjogren’s syndrome compared to those with secondary Sjogren’s syndrome.

JACUSA is a software solution designed to detect single nucleotide variants by comparing data from next-generation sequencing experiments, specifically RNA-DNA or RNA-RNA samples. It demonstrates higher recall and comparable precision when detecting A→I sites from RNA-DNA comparisons, and higher precision and recall in RNA-RNA comparisons. JACUSA was evaluated against other algorithms and outperformed them in benchmark scenarios, particularly in RNA-RNA comparisons, and can identify site-specific RNA editing events. It is a versatile tool for detecting single nucleotide variant positions.

Tezepelumab is a human monoclonal antibody that functions by blocking thymic stromal lymphopoietin, a cytokine involved in the development of asthma. It significantly reduces exacerbations in adults with severe asthma, and its effects on asthma exacerbation rate and fractional exhaled nitric oxide levels are related to its pharmacokinetic properties. Subcutaneous dosing at 210 mg every 4 weeks is predicted to achieve approximately 90% of the maximum drug effect of tezepelumab.

Kidney donation is associated with elevated risks of gestational hypertension and pre-eclampsia. Studies show a higher incidence of these conditions in postdonation pregnancies, with rates of gestational hypertension increasing from 0.6% to 5.7% and preeclampsia from 0.8% to 5.5%. Additionally, postdonation pregnancies may also incur attributable risk of gestational diabetes and proteinuria.

Yes, hyperprolactinemia due to prolactinoma is a major risk factor for osteoporosis, with women with prolactinoma having a 4.5 times increased relative risk of developing the condition. Bone loss is significantly correlated with disease duration and hypogonadism in patients with prolactinoma, and osteoporosis was observed in cases presenting with chronic anaemia.
The clinical trial phase for the drug SYL040012 is not mentioned in the provided text.

The RNA Centric Annotation System (RCAS) is an R package designed to simplify the creation of gene-centric annotations and analysis for genomic regions of interest obtained from RNA-based omics technologies. Its modular design allows for flexible use and integration with other bioinformatics workflows, and it is accessible through various interfaces including a Galaxy wrapper and a web service. RCAS provides meta-gene profiles, gene-centric annotation, motif analysis, and gene-set analysis to offer contextual knowledge for understanding biological events involving RNAs, and has been shown to reproduce published findings while also generating novel hypotheses. It is available at http://bioconductor.org/packages/release/bioc/html/RCAS.html and http://rcas.mdc-berlin.de.

Chronic Wasting Disease is a fatal and transmissible spongiform encephalopathy affecting cervids like white-tailed deer, mule deer, elk, and moose. It is an emerging prion disease that can be spread through shedding infectious prions in bodily fluids like saliva and urine. CWD leads to neurodegeneration and death, and can contribute to significant population declines in affected deer species, with a 100% fatality rate.

A1C is a measure of glycemic control and a surrogate for mean plasma glucose. It can be used to assess the quality of care for patients with diabetes, incorporating domains like effectiveness and equity. Higher A1C levels are associated with increased risk of adverse outcomes, such as abnormal glucose postpartum and may reflect psychosocial barriers to adherence in adolescents with type 1 diabetes.

Yes, Down syndrome occurs when an individual has all or part of an extra copy of chromosome 21, as stated in descriptions of the disorder and its genetic basis. Several instances detail the condition arising from an extra copy of chromosome 21 or a segment of it.

Craniosynostosis is a defect of the skull caused by the premature fusion of one or more of the cranial sutures. This condition affects approximately 3 to 5 individuals per 10,000 live births, though it can occur in about 1 to 2,000 live births as well. It can be classified as either syndromic, associated with other anomalies and developmental delay, or nonsyndromic, appearing as an isolated finding.

McEnhancer is a machine learning approach that links target genes to putative enhancers via a semi-supervised learning algorithm predicting gene expression patterns based on enriched sequence features, while EMBER integrates binding and expression data via an unsupervised machine learning algorithm for inferring gene targets of transcription factor binding sites.

Click-PEGylation is a method used to robustly and flexibly modify proteins by selectively tagging redox-modified cysteine residues with polyethylene glycol (PEG). It involves first labeling free thiol groups with a reagent containing an alkyne, then reacting this with a PEG molecule containing an azide function through a Click chemistry approach. Separating the thiol labeling and PEG conjugation steps improves the fidelity of the process and allows for the study of reversibly reduced or oxidized cysteines, ultimately enabling interrogation of a protein’s redox state. This technique can also be adapted to degrade RNA species by covalently attaching small molecules through click-chemistry, similar to ribonucleases.

The Systemic Lupus Erythematosus Disease Activity Index (SLEDAI) is a scoring system used to assess disease activity in Systemic Lupus Erythematosus. A score of greater than or equal to 10 is considered active and severe disease, while a score of 6 or more indicates eligibility for certain clinical trials. Clinicians use SLEDAI scores to describe changes in disease activity and to identify flares, with increases of a median of 4 indicating a flare.

The ALK tyrosine kinase is associated with non-small cell lung cancer, anaplastic large cell lymphoma, inflammatory myofibroblastic tumors, neuroblastoma, and renal medullary carcinoma, among other cancers.
CURB65 score is used for stratification of patients with COPD exacerbation and community acquired pneumonia.
Stable transgenic mouse lines that express CreER have been generated as a data and tool resource to study transcriptional regulation of GABAergic cell fate.

Yes, creatinine assessment is included in the MELD score calculation, as the formula provided states MELD = 3.8*LN[total bilirubin] + 9.6*LN[creatinine] + 11.2*[PT-INR] + 6.4. Additionally, several studies evaluate modifications to the MELD score based on creatinine levels to improve its prognostic accuracy.
Synaptophysin and rab 3A are the most down-regulated synaptic proteins, and acetylcholine and GABA were significantly changed in Alzheimer’s disease subjects compared to controls.

A selective estrogen receptor modulator is a synthetic ligand for estrogen receptors exhibiting tissue-specific agonistic/antagonistic activities, and can also be described as a compound with transcriptional activity that modulates estrogen receptors.

Yes, celiac disease is caused by gliadin-induced transglutaminase-2 (TG2)-dependent events. The information indicates that TG2 deamidates gliadin peptides, enhancing their immunogenicity and provoking an autoimmune response where TG2 is the main autoantigen. Additionally, TG2 modifies proteins and peptides by transamidation or deamidation of glutamine residues, playing a central role in the pathogenesis of the disease. The deamidation of gliadin peptides by TG2 is important in the immunopathogenesis of celiac disease.
The Limb-Enhancer Genie (LEG) is a collection of highly accurate, genome-wide predictions of enhancers in the developing limb, available through a user-friendly online interface.
Corticosteroids are consistently identified as the first-line therapy for sarcoidosis.
Dialectical behavior therapy is used to treat borderline personality disorder, as well as comorbid personality disorders such as paranoid and obsessive-compulsive personality disorder.

Okazaki fragments are associated with lagging strand DNA synthesis, which requires the removal of RNA primers, gap filling, and ligation to complete DNA replication and is vital for cell proliferation.

No, wheat and oats are distinct plants studied in relation to each other. Several documents mention both wheat (Triticum aestivum L.) and oats (Avena sativa L.) as separate species, investigating interactions like allelopathy or comparing gravitropism and genetic mapping between them. One document even details isolating compounds from wild oats and testing their effects on wheat, indicating they are not the same genus.
Clue cells are associated with Gardnerella vaginalis, as well as with Mycoplasma hominis and Ureaplasma urealyticum.

The fourth generation HIV test includes the detection of anti-HIV antibodies and HIV p24 antigen, and newer versions may provide separate antigen and antibody results while some combine both into a single assay.
De Quervain’s disease is diagnosed using the Finkelstein’s test, which is considered pathognomonic for the condition and is a classic diagnostic test.

Uc.189 can be used as a potential prognostic biomarker for esophageal squamous cell carcinomas, gynecological cancers including cervical squamous cell carcinomas and endometrial adenocarcinomas, and potentially ovarian cystoadenocarcinomas.

No, Uc.160 appears to suppress tumor growth. It is downregulated in gastric cancer tissues and cell lines, and over-expression inhibits proliferation. Additionally, it potentially has a tumor suppressive role in gastric cancer by regulating PTEN expression and inhibiting Akt phosphorylation. Cancer-specific DNA methylation downregulates its expression.
The provided text discusses colorectal cancer and maternal health inequities, and does not contain information about the causes of Black Lung.
The four principles of medical ethics are beneficence, respect for autonomy, non-maleficence, and justice.

Diffloop is an R/Bioconductor package that provides functions for the quality control, statistical testing, annotation, and visualization of DNA loops to systematically assess changes in DNA looping architecture between samples.
The “probe to bone” test can be used to diagnose osteomyelitis, particularly in the foot of patients with diabetes.

Yes, doxycycline can cause photosensitivity. Several instances mention photosensitivity reactions occurring in patients taking doxycycline, and one study specifically investigates drug-induced photosensitivity including cases linked to doxycycline use. Additionally, a formulation of doxycycline is contraindicated in individuals with increased photosensitivity.
Dentate gyrus mossy cells control signal processing in learning and memory, as well as potentially contribute to seizure propagation and network hyperexcitability.
The posterior cruciate ligament is most commonly injured in dashboard injuries.
The median normal body temperature in dogs, as measured by an experienced investigator using a rectal digital thermometer, is 38.3°C.

Heerfordt syndrome presents with facial nerve palsy, parotid gland enlargement or swelling, uveitis, fever—which can be low-grade or high—and, less commonly, radiculopathy or bilateral hearing loss, sometimes accompanied by swelling of lymph nodes.
The stifle joint is composed of the femur, tibia, and patella bones, along with articular cartilage, menisci, and ligaments.

Yes, paralog genes are co-regulated. Several instances demonstrate this, including their coordinated expression according to topologically associating domains and shared common regulatory mechanisms, as well as enriched co-localization within the same TAD and common enhancer elements. Additionally, co-regulated pairs of testis-biased genes were identified, and regions of co-regulation (RIDGEs) were found to consist of paralog genes.
Infliximab, a monoclonal antibody to tumor necrosis factor-alpha, and vedolizumab, an anti-α4β7 integrin, are two approved drug treatments for Inflammatory Bowel Disease.
FullSSR is a bioinformatic tool for microsatellite (SSR) loci detection and primer design using genomic data from NGS assay.

CistromeMap, CR Cistrome, Cistrome Data Browser, and GeneProf Data are curated resources for ChIP-seq data, providing comprehensive knowledgebases, databases, and collections of analyzed experiments for studying chromatin regulators, histone modifications, and gene regulation.
The Gore REDUCE Clinical Study investigated treatment for patients who had a cryptogenic stroke.

StereoGene’s applicability in regulatory genomics is demonstrated through numerous comparisons of ChIP-Seq datasets from the Human Epigenome Atlas and FANTOM CAGE, as well as observations of changes in correlation between epigenomic features across developmental trajectories of several tissue types and the discovery of a novel spatial correlation of CAGE clusters with donor splice sites and poly(A) sites.

The 4D Nucleome Network seeks to map the structure and dynamics of genomes in space and time. This project aims to gain mechanistic insights into how the nucleus is organized and functions. It will develop and test experimental and computational methods for measuring genome conformation and nuclear organization, and investigate their contributions to gene regulation and other genome functions.

Zieve's syndrome presents with jaundice, hyperlipaemia, hemolytic anemia, and potentially fatty liver, hyperlipoproteinemia, and milky turbidity of the blood alongside hepatomegaly and anaemia of internal organs.

Symptoms of anxiety and depression are frequently observed in patients with psoriatic arthritis, and baseline depression/anxiety can negatively predict remission in psoriatic arthritis as well as being associated with increased patient and evaluator global assessments.

No, polyadenylation promotes degradation of mRNAs. It involves the addition of adenosine residues to the 3' end of mRNA, and this process accelerates mRNA degradation in plants and contributes to the destabilization of bacterial mRNA. While essential for eukaryotic mRNA expression, it does not appear to stabilize the protein itself.
Carney's triad includes multifocal pulmonary chondroma, gastric stromal sarcoma or epithelioid leiomyosarcoma, and extra-adrenal paraganglioma.

The preDIVA trial is a cluster-randomized trial—with a 6-year follow-up involving elderly subjects aged 70 to 78—designed to assess whether nurse-led intensive vascular care in primary care decreases the incidence of dementia and reduces disability, and it includes a multidomain intervention targeting cardiovascular risk factors such as hypertension, hypercholesterolemia, diabetes, overweight, smoking, and physical exercise.
Azeliragon is an inhibitor of the receptor for advanced glycation end products (RAGE).
Forxiga, also known as dapagliflozin, is used for the treatment of type 2 diabetes and may potentially play a role in treating diabetes-associated neurological disorders like Alzheimer's disease.
The most common feature of Doege-Potter syndrome is hypoglycemia resulting from tumors, most often solitary fibrous tumors of the pleura, producing insulin growth factor-like II.
Milwaukee protocol was tested for treatment of rabies encephalitis and rabies infection in humans and canines.

No, stress granules are described as non-membranous structures that transiently form in the cytoplasm during cellular stress. They are assemblies of mRNA and protein that facilitate an adaptive cellular response to environmental adversity.

16S rRNA gene sequencing is used to analyze bacterial communities and assess their diversity, both within and between samples. It can be applied to study microbiota in various environments, including the human intestine, skin lesions, and even soil samples from crime scenes. This technique involves amplifying and sequencing a specific region of the 16S rRNA gene to identify and compare taxa present in different samples, and can be performed using platforms like Illumina MiSeq.
Intepirdine targets the 5-HT6 receptor.

No, evidence suggests chocolate consumption may offer protective effects against cardiovascular disease. Studies indicate daily consumption of dark chocolate can reduce cardiovascular events and is associated with improvements in factors like arterial stiffness, insulin resistance, and blood pressure, potentially lowering cardiovascular disease risk. Chocolate increases plasma antioxidant capacity, diminishes platelet function and inflammation, and decreases arterial pressures.

Circular RNA is a key regulator in the development and progression of human cancers, and specifically, circ-ABCB10 is significantly up-regulated in breast cancer tissue. These circular RNAs can function as microRNA sponges and are involved in essential processes like cell proliferation. Studies have also contributed to molecular profiling tests, like Oncotype Dx, to identify patients who may benefit from hormonal therapy alone, and are exploring their potential as biomarkers or therapeutic targets in the disease.
Wernicke encephalopathy presents with changes in mental status, ataxia, and ocular abnormalities such as nystagmus or ophthalmoplegia.

RIblast is an ultrafast RNA-RNA interaction prediction method utilizing a seed-and-extension approach. It identifies seed regions with suffix arrays and extends them based on RNA secondary structure energy modeling. This method achieves prediction accuracy comparable to existing programs, but operates over 64 times faster, making it suitable for large-scale lncRNA datasets. The source code for RIblast is freely available online.

Alzheimer’s disease resilience refers to performing at a high and sustained level despite genetic risks associated with the disease, such as those related to the APOE ɛ4 and CLU genes. It can be measured by examining the difference between expected outcomes based on Alzheimer’s disease pathology biomarkers and actual brain aging outcomes like hippocampal volume and cognition. Individuals demonstrating resilience exhibit slower rates of cognitive decline and a decreased risk of diagnostic conversion to Alzheimer’s disease.
RAPIDR, an open-source R package, has been developed for implementing several published NIPT analysis methods and includes quality control steps for clinical use.

Yes, CDKN2A appears to be nevogenic. Several instances demonstrate that individuals carrying CDKN2A mutations have increased nevus numbers and densities compared to non-carriers, and family members with the atypical mole syndrome are more likely to be mutant gene carriers. Continued accumulation of nevi in mutation carriers further supports this role, and the gene is described as having a nevogenic effect.

Yes, a Bayesian model called RiVIERA (Risk Variant Inference using Epigenomic Reference Annotations) exists for joint Bayesian inference of driver variants from summary statistics across multiple traits. This model was applied to jointly harness potential causal enrichments among tissue-specific epigenomic annotations and further improved detection power in simulations.

Achenbach’s syndrome, also known as paroxysmal finger hematoma, causes sudden bruising on one or more fingers, often without any prior injury. It is a rarely reported condition that primarily affects women, with a median age of around 49.5 years, and is typically self-limiting, resolving within a few days without intervention. While it can be alarming due to intense pain, edema, and sometimes paresthesia, it is a benign and non-urgent condition that doesn’t require surgical intervention.

Yes, intepirdine has been evaluated in clinical trials, including a Phase 3 study called MINDSET involving 1315 patients with mild-to-moderate Alzheimer’s disease dementia who were already taking donepezil. Earlier phase trials, including a phase 2b study, also supported its use as a potential therapy. However, larger phase-III trials ultimately failed to demonstrate a statistically significant impact on cognition.

No, new work weakens the possible links between the measles vaccine and subacute sclerosing panencephalitis, and the success of reducing the problem is attributed to general measles vaccination with high coverage. While some reports exist linking SSPE to vaccination, the information suggests the disease is primarily caused by the wild measles virus.

Host-induced gene silencing is a transgenic technology that silences fungal genes within a plant during infection, reducing disease levels. This process relies on the plant’s production of small interfering RNA molecules, generated from long double-stranded RNA, which complement targeted fungal genes. These molecules are transferred to invading fungi to cause gene silencing through an uncharacterised mechanism. It is a method used to control plant pathogenic fungi by regulating gene expression.

Maternal spindle transfer is one of two embryonic techniques enacted in the UK to prevent the transmission of serious mitochondrial diseases. It involves transferring nuclear DNA from an egg containing defective mitochondria to another egg with normal mitochondria. This technique, along with pronuclear transfer and polar body transfer, falls under the umbrella of mitochondrial replacement therapy and aims to avoid passing on pathogenic mtDNA to future generations.

No, an eight-week course of armodafinil did not improve fatigue in glioma patients undergoing radiotherapy in a pilot study. Further analysis indicated no difference between those receiving armodafinil and those receiving a placebo, even when accounting for missing data.

Tankyrases are recruited to DNA lesions by MDC1 and regulate DNA end resection and BRCA1A complex stabilization. This leads to efficient DSB repair by Homologous Recombination and proper checkpoint activation. They function as novel binding partners of MDC1 in the cellular response to DSBs, contributing to genome stability. Ultimately, tankyrases promote homologous recombination when DSBs occur.

Dovitinib inhibits topoisomerase I, topoisomerase IIα, the DNA-independent ATPase activity of yeast topoisomerase II, FGFR-1, FRS2-α, ERK1/2, FGFR/PDGFR-β/VEGFR-2 signaling pathways, p-cdk-2, and cyclin B1, while also impacting STAT3 through SHP-1 dependent inhibition.

CircRNAs are dysregulated in cancer and play key roles in tumorigenesis, impacting processes like cell proliferation, apoptosis, and metastasis. They can function as competing endogenous RNAs or microRNA sponges, regulating transcription and splicing, and also by binding to proteins and influencing translation.  These molecules are implicated in the bioprocesses of carcinogenesis and have been correlated with clinical characteristics and grade in cancer progression, specifically breast cancer. Overall, circRNAs have emerged as important regulators in gene expression that impact many physiological processes, including tumorigenesis.

Genomiser is an analysis framework able to score the relevance of variation in the non-coding genome and associate regulatory variants to specific Mendelian diseases through existing methods like CADD or a bespoke machine learning method, combined with allele frequency, regulatory sequences, chromosomal topological domains, and phenotypic relevance.

No, treatment with pregabalin did not significantly reduce leg pain intensity associated with sciatica compared to placebo over eight weeks. Evidence suggests pregabalin and gabapentin demonstrate comparable efficacy, but the quality of evidence is low and no direct comparison studies exist.

Yes, gas vesicles are detectable at subnanomolar concentrations and serve as highly sensitive imaging agents for ultrasound, and are identified as nanoscale reporters for ultrasound imaging. They can be imaged with ultrasound in vitro and in vivo using optimized pulse sequences.

Crenezumab is being tested in clinical trials, including secondary prevention trials in presymptomatic subjects with autosomal dominant Alzheimer's disease mutations, and is a new passive anti-Aβ immunotherapy under clinical testing.
A novel branch point prediction algorithm called BPP, which utilizes information on the branch point sequence and the polypyrimidine tract, has been proposed.
Streptococcus pyogenes, a beta-hemolytic group A Streptococcus, causes scarlet fever and is also known as the aetiological agent of scarletina.
The BioArchive system is an automated system used by umbilical cord blood banks to freeze and store cord blood for clinical transplantation.
SeqArray is an algorithm implemented in the R/Bioconductor package for storing variant calls in an array-oriented manner with high compression options and parallel computing data access.

Yes, Citrobacter rodentium is pathogenic as it is described as a mouse pathogen that causes diarrheal diseases and is used to induce colitis in mice, triggering inflammatory responses and colonic pathology. It is also used as a model for enteropathogenic Escherichia coli (EPEC) infection.
Actoxumab and bezlotoxumab are two human monoclonal antibodies against Clostridium difficile toxins A and B, respectively.

Ribosomal biogenesis involves proteins such as RPL5, RPL11, RPL12, RPS19, and nucleostemin, as well as HEATR1 which positively regulates ribosomal RNA synthesis, and various other ribosomal proteins that contribute to the assembly of ribosomal subunits.
Tudor interacting repair regulator (TIRR) regulates p53-binding protein 1 (53BP1).

ExpansionHunter and repeat expansion detection (RED) are algorithms used for the detection of long repeat expansions, with ExpansionHunter Denovo being a catalog-free method for genome-wide repeat expansion detection.
Neuroligin-3 promotes the growth of high-grade gliomas, including adult and paediatric glioblastoma, anaplastic oligodendroglioma, and diffuse intrinsic pontine glioma.
The iLIR database has been developed as a freely available web resource listing all putative canonical LIRCPs identified in silico in the proteomes of 8 model organisms.

Liquid-liquid phase transition is a reversible process resulting in the formation of condensed liquid droplets or microcompartments, driven by factors like crowding and electrostatic interactions, and is crucial for the formation of both intracellular organelles and soluble aggregates.

Yes, CD55 deficiency can cause thrombosis. Loss-of-function mutations in the gene encoding CD55 lead to abnormal complement activation, angiopathic thrombosis, and protein-losing enteropathy, a condition known as CHAPLE syndrome. Additionally, thrombosis is a prominent feature in paroxysmal nocturnal hemoglobinuria, which is characterized by a deficiency of GPI-anchored proteins like CD55. Eculizumab, a treatment that reduces complement activation, also reduces thrombotic risk, suggesting a role for complement activation in thrombosis related to CD55 deficiency.
Gas vesicles attain a maximum diameter of 100 nm during formation.

Yes, sleep apnea is associated with cardiac arrhythmias, as patients with sleep apnea have a higher prevalence of them than nonapneic patients. However, snoring alone, without sleep apnea, is not associated with an increased frequency of cardiac arrhythmias; the prevalence of arrhythmias was similar in light and heavy snorers. Both obstructive and central sleep apnea are linked to an increased risk of cardiac arrhythmia and may contribute to sudden cardiac death.

Yes, osteoclasts are normal bone-resorbing cells that cause bone degradation, and osteoclast-mediated bone degradation leads to the release of factors promoting skeletal tumor growth. They also stimulate tumor growth and are involved in bone remodeling.

BET proteins include BRD2, BRD3, and BRD4, and the foamy virus Bet protein functions as an inhibitor of APOBEC3 family proteins while another Bet protein is derived from an African green monkey foamy virus.

Yes, temsirolimus may improve survival in some glioblastoma patients. One study showed 38 of 54 patients treated with temsirolimus reached 12-month overall survival, with a 1-year survival rate of 72.2%. Additionally, temsirolimus improved overall survival among patients with metastatic renal-cell carcinoma and a poor prognosis compared to interferon alone, with a median overall survival of 10.9 months. However, other studies indicate it does not improve overall survival when used in combination with other treatments or as a single agent in glioblastoma.

A proteoform is a protein product with various primary structure alterations resulting from gene mutations, alternative splicing, and post-translational modifications, representing the chemical diversity of proteins and expressing their properties.

Yes, the analysis reveals sex differences in the transcriptome of the developing mouse hippocampus, with 198 transcripts differentially expressed between females and males at one or more ages, and this number is greater in adult animals than in younger animals. Additionally, 69 transcripts show complex and sex-specific patterns of temporal regulation through postnatal development.
Neuronal activity induces the secretion of neuroligin-3, and this secretion promotes glioma proliferation through the PI3K-mTOR pathway.

The blood-brain barrier protects the central nervous system from harmful agents found in the blood. It also presents an obstacle to delivering medications to the brain, but drug candidates can be designed to cross it or utilize delivery systems to facilitate passage. Disruption of this barrier plays a key role in several central nervous system disorders, particularly those involving neurodegeneration, and can lead to inflammation as immune cells gain access to the brain. Maintaining the barrier’s integrity is important for preserving the brain as an “immune sanctuary.”
Sclerostin regulates bone growth, remodeling, repair, mass, resorption, apposition, and the differentiation and/or function of osteoblasts.

PARP inhibitor resistance occurs when cancer cells acquire new mutations or events that overcome the drugs’ positive effects. This resistance is a growing concern in clinical settings, and can develop even in cells with BRCA mutations. Changes in PARP-1 activity or expression may contribute to this resistance, and overcoming it is a current focus of research through strategies like combining PARPi with other inhibitors.
RAB10 is regulated by miRNA-655 and is involved in the biological processes of protein transport and regulation of cell motion, potentially making it a biomarker of Alzheimer’s disease.
No, MC1R palmitoylation enhances pigmentation. It triggers increased pigmentation in vitro and in vivo and is essential for activating MC1R signalling, which is linked to melanin production.

Yes, a link has been discovered between the bite of the Lone Star Tick and the development of sensitivity to galactose-α-1,3-galactose, a carbohydrate found in beef, pork, and lamb, leading to a red meat allergy.

pQTLs are genomic loci that influence physiological and pathophysiological traits, such as systolic blood pressure and blood glucose. They can colocalize with cis-eQTLs, making them attractive as primary susceptibility loci. These loci are identified through quantitative trait locus analysis and can help elucidate the molecular basis of complex traits and diseases.

Dysphoric Milk Ejection Reflex (D-MER) is an abrupt emotional shift, ranging from wistfulness to self-loathing, that occurs in some women just before milk release and lasts only a few minutes. It results in waves of negative emotions that begin shortly before milk ejection and continue until the baby begins to feed. This condition appears to have a physiological cause, potentially linked to a temporary drop in dopamine, and typically resolves after milk ejection, though it can persist throughout breastfeeding.

Yes, rucaparib demonstrates efficacy in treating ovarian cancer. Clinical trials show overall response rates of 80% in patients with deleterious BRCA1/2 mutations and activity even in BRCA wild types with high loss of heterozygosity. It is approved as monotherapy for advanced ovarian cancer associated with deleterious BRCA mutations and is well-tolerated, with ongoing studies further exploring its use.

The protein inference problem involves determining which candidate proteins are present in a sample. It aims to select a subset of proteins that best explains the observed peptides, and is a major computational challenge in mass spectrometry-based proteomics. This problem can be viewed as a special case of protein quantification, where present proteins have non-zero abundance values, and is often complicated by issues like peptide degeneracy and homologous protein sequences. Several approaches attempt to solve this, including those based on peptide detectability, Lasso regression, and linear programming.

Early studies suggested a strong association between maternal lithium treatment during pregnancy and Ebstein’s anomaly, with some estimates indicating a significantly increased risk. However, more recent and controlled studies have consistently shown a lower risk, and some case-control studies have found no link between lithium use and Ebstein’s anomaly. Despite this, fetal echocardiography is advisable to exclude the possibility of cardiac anomalies in pregnancies where lithium is used, as some infants exposed to lithium have been identified with the condition.
No, verubecestat is described as an oral BACE-1 inhibitor that reduces amyloid-beta levels, and blocks production of amyloid-beta. It is designed to inhibit, not activate, BACE-1.

The Ribosome-bound Quality Control complex recognizes aberrant peptides translated from defective mRNAs and polyubiquitylates them. It then extracts these peptides from stalled 60S ribosomal subunits and delivers them to the proteasome for degradation. This process prevents toxicity that may arise from the aggregation of defective polypeptides and maintains proteostasis.
Emicizumab is a bispecific antibody recognizing both factor IXa and factor X that mimics activated factor VIII cofactor function and promotes colocalization of the enzyme-substrate complex.

The Lone Star tick, Amblyomma americanum, transmits Ehrlichia chaffeensis causing human monocytic ehrlichiosis, tularemia, and is associated with southern tick-associated rash illness (STARI) which presents with Lyme-like lesions, and has historically been reported to transmit Rocky Mountain spotted fever rickettsiae, though its role in these illnesses ranges from incidental to significant.
Ubiquilins function as ubiquitin receptors and adaptor proteins involved in proteasomal degradation, autophagosome maturation, and the crosstalk among proteolytic pathways.

DeepLoc is a prediction algorithm using deep neural networks with a recurrent neural network and attention mechanism that predicts protein subcellular localization relying only on sequence information.
The brown marmorated stink bug originates from Asia, specifically including China, Taiwan, Japan, and the Korean peninsula, and is also indigenous to northeastern Asia.

Scarlet fever presents with a distinctive macro-papular rash, fever, and potentially exudate and cervical adenopathy, and can be associated with group C beta-hemolytic streptococcal pharyngitis while hemolytic streptococcus is present in the throats of patients during the first week of the disease and may be carried by healthy individuals.
Gallbladder carriage is a well recognised means of spread of Salmonella Typhi, which causes typhoid fever.
The Mentzer index includes the ratio of mean corpuscular volume to red blood cell count, and a decision point for this index has been determined to be a ratio of 20.
Fexinidazole is used to treat human African trypanosomiasis (sleeping sickness) and Chagas disease.

Yes, tocilizumab is effective for Giant Cell Arteritis. It has demonstrated efficacy in multiple patients, allowing for a reduction in glucocorticoid dosage and showing positive outcomes even in cases resistant to corticosteroids. Studies report it is effective in all but a few patients, and observational evidence supports its use, particularly in refractory cases.

Yes, nuclear position dictates the choice of DNA repair pathway. Double-strand breaks induced at the nuclear membrane fail to rapidly activate the DNA damage response and repair by homologous recombination, instead being repaired by alternative end-joining in situ, revealing a new level of regulation controlled by spatial organization of DNA within the nucleus.

No. The provided information details mitophagy as a process heavily reliant on ubiquitination – specifically, the recruitment of Parkin following PINK1 accumulation on damaged mitochondria, leading to the ubiquitination of outer mitochondrial membrane proteins. This ubiquitination is essential for associating mitochondria with autophagosome formation and subsequent degradation. While Tomm20 is a mitochondrial protein, there is no mention of its involvement in mitophagy independent of ubiquitination, and the described mechanism consistently emphasizes ubiquitination as a key initiating step.

No, neurons from mutant mouse strains with SARM1 deletion exhibited secretion of fibrils after anterograde transport, indicating that trans-neuronal spread can occur in intact, healthy neurons, and therefore SARM1 deletion does not cause neurodegeneration in this instance.

Yes, DNA polymerase θ is involved in the repair of genomic double-strand breaks from many sources, including those formed by ionizing radiation, topoisomerase inhibitors, stalled DNA replication forks, and CRISPR-Cas9, and participates in a repair route termed “alternative end-joining.” It also appears to be a key player in PARP-mediated DNA damage repair and essential for the survival of cancer cells where homologous recombination is compromised.

Several polypill formulations include ramipril, hydrochlorothiazide, atenolol, aspirin, simvastatin, lisinopril, and hydrochlorothiazide, while another combination consists of amlodipine besylate and atorvastatin calcium.
The suprachiasmatic nuclei of the hypothalamus controls the circadian clock as a master clock, synchronizing peripheral tissues and directly orchestrating circadian physiology.

Several algorithms are used for compression of SAM files, including GeneComp, SAMZIP, CSAM, QVZ, and NGC, which employ both lossless and lossy compression techniques to reduce file size and improve transmission efficiency.
The context does not contain information about what MetaHIT stands for.
Morphine, methadone, buprenorphine, and naloxone are four drugs used in the treatment of opioid addiction or to reverse the effects of an overdose.

Yes, organic semiconducting nanoparticles (OSNs) with ultralong phosphorescence can be used for in vivo afterglow imaging, as they greatly stabilize triplet excited states and permit imaging of lymph nodes in living mice with a high signal-to-noise ratio. This eliminates the need for light excitation and circumvents autofluorescence, holding promise for molecular imaging.

Romosozumab is a humanized monoclonal antibody to sclerostin, a glycoprotein that blocks bone formation. By binding to sclerostin, romosozumab permits engagement of Wnt ligands, which increases bone formation and bone mineral density. Additionally, romosozumab uniquely suppresses bone resorption while increasing bone formation, offering a dual effect on bone volume regulation.

Nrf2 regulates the expression of proteins involved in the detoxification and elimination of reactive oxidants and electrophiles. Specifically, Nrf2 upregulates heme oxygenase-1 and other antioxidant enzymes, and it induces the transcriptional repressor BACH1. Additionally, Nrf2 regulates thioredoxin reductase 1, although this regulation differs from its effect on heme oxygenase-1.
Sofosbuvir-based therapy and ledipasvir/sofosbuvir are drugs that cure hepatitis C virus infection.

Yes, IL-11 is a crucial determinant of cardiovascular fibrosis, and its inhibition prevents fibroblast activation across organs in response to pro-fibrotic stimuli. Upregulation of IL-11 is dominant in the transcriptional response to TGFβ1 exposure and is required for its pro-fibrotic effect. Fibroblast-specific Il11 transgene expression or Il-11 injection causes heart and kidney fibrosis and organ failure.

Major risk factors for Alzheimer's disease include hypertension, cerebrovascular impairment, carrying a single E4 allele of apolipoprotein E, mutations in the amyloid precursor protein, presenilin 1, or presenilin 2 genes, and mutations in the hemochromatosis gene.

Wells criteria can reliably risk stratify pretest probability in patients with suspected pulmonary embolism and predict prevalence rates of 2%, 15%, and 43% for low, moderate, and high pretest probability, respectively, when trichotomized, or 3% and 28% when dichotomized.

Tabix is a tool designed to quickly retrieve sequence features from position-sorted, TAB-delimited files like GFF, BED, and SAM. It indexes these files to enable fast access to overlapping features within specified regions. This tool is useful for examining genomic features and supports large data files and remote custom tracks, functioning as both a command-line tool and a library in several programming languages.

MULTOVL is an application suite that detects and statistically analyzes multiple overlaps of genomic regions quickly and efficiently. It supports identifying intersections, unions, and solitary genomic regions. The significance of observed overlaps is determined by comparing them to empirical null distributions created through random shuffling of input regions.

Having at least one A-allele of HSD17B1 is linked to a significantly increased risk of endometriosis. Specifically, individuals with either an A/G or A/A genotype demonstrate a higher risk, and these genotypes also correlate with the severity of the condition. The A-allele of HSD17B1 appears to increase the risk for endometriosis, as evidenced by research in a Japanese population.
Tecfidera is used for the treatment of relapsing-remitting multiple sclerosis and relapsing forms of multiple sclerosis.

Yes, Exploding Head Syndrome is a recognized condition characterized by the perception of loud noises while falling asleep or waking up, and sometimes described as an explosive noise in the head. It is considered a parasomnia and, while frightening, is generally benign and self-limited. It can also occur in individuals with epilepsy and may be responsive to medications like topiramate or clobazam.

INCB3619 is a potent, orally bioavailable small-molecule inhibitor of a subset of ADAM proteases that blocks the shedding of ErbB ligands. It selectively inhibits ErbB ligand shedding and prevents the processing and activation of multiple ErbB ligands, including heregulin. This inhibition reduces ErbB pathway signaling, tumor cell proliferation, and survival, and it can synergize with other cancer therapeutics.

Crenezumab is a humanized antibody that targets Amyloid-β (Aβ) and is currently being tested in clinical trials for Alzheimer’s disease prevention. It detects plaques, cerebral amyloid angiopathy, and intraneuronal Aβ, and also recognizes N-terminally modified Aβ peptides. Along with solanezumab and bapineuzumab, it is a biosimilar antibody with a surprisingly similar staining profile across different mouse models.

Vanishing lung syndrome is a rare radiological and clinical condition characterized by giant emphysematous bullae, often in the upper lobes of the lungs, which can make the lungs appear to disappear on X-ray. It is a chronic, progressive disease typically affecting young male smokers, though cases have been reported in women and appear to have a genetic component with autosomal dominant inheritance observed in some families. The syndrome is associated with significant morbidity and mortality, and can lead to complications like pneumothorax, respiratory failure, and bronchopleural fistulas, but can be treated with bullectomy or, in some cases, a medical pneumonectomy.
The trispecific HIV antibodies target the CD4 binding site, the membrane-proximal external region (MPER), and the V1V2 glycan site of the HIV-1 envelope.

Calciphylaxis is a rare and potentially life-threatening condition causing skin necrosis, characterized by painful purple skin lesions that can progress to ulcerations and necrosis. It involves calcification, thrombosis, and cutaneous ischemia in the skin and subcutaneous tissue, and is often associated with a high mortality rate. While typically linked to end-stage renal disease and abnormal calcium metabolism, it can occur in patients with normal renal function, and may be related to medications like warfarin.

Alternative lengthening of telomeres is a telomerase independent mechanism for maintaining telomere length that occurs in approximately 15% of cancers. It involves homology-directed telomere synthesis, and can result in excessively long telomeric overhangs with preferential elongation of lagging strands. This pathway is associated with chromosomal instability and can trigger innate immune signaling, and its maintenance may involve the FA pathway and FANCD2 protein.

Fibroblast growth factor 21 is an endocrine metabolic regulator expressed in multiple tissues, including the liver and adipose tissue, with the highest levels of expression found in the pancreas. The liver primarily produces this hormone, which is a naturally occurring peptide. FGF21 receptors are also found in pancreatic tissues, skeletal muscles, and adipose tissues, allowing it to influence metabolic processes through endocrine cellular effects.

Neddylation is a post-translational protein modification involving the conjugation of NEDD8 to target proteins, regulating diverse cellular processes like protein degradation, transcription, and cell signaling. It controls protein function by altering conformation, stability, or binding affinity, and is linked to various diseases including cancer, neurodegenerative disorders, and cardiac disease. Furthermore, neddylation plays a role in regulating the activity of E3 ligases and can modulate vascular endothelial function through alterations in protein degradation.

Break-induced replication is a pathway specializing in the repair of one-ended double-strand DNA breaks, often arising from collapsed replication forks or eroded telomeres. It begins with a broken DNA end invading a homologous template, initiating DNA synthesis that can extend for hundreds of kilobases. Unlike typical replication, this process proceeds via a migrating bubble and results in conservative inheritance of newly synthesized DNA, though it is associated with genetic instabilities like hyper-mutagenesis and chromosomal rearrangements. Microhomology-mediated break-induced replication can specifically promote complex chromosomal rearrangements.

Fibrocytes are unique hematopoietic-derived stem cells that play a pivotal role in wound healing and help form scar tissue alongside fibroblasts. They are spindle-shaped, fibroblast-like cells that can promote collagen production and are increased in fibrotic tissues. Fibrocytes can differentiate from monocytes and are mobilized during negative pressure wound therapy, potentially aiding in the healing of intractable wounds.

lncRNA H19 affects cardiac differentiation, proliferation, apoptosis, autophagy, mineralization, and left ventricular function, and it modulates the expression of genes such as Nkx-2.5, GATA4, α-MHC, MLC-2v, Sox6, NOTCH1, RUNX2, BMP2, DIRAS3, miR-19b, miR-675, and PA2G4.
Dinutuximab is used for the treatment of high-risk neuroblastoma in pediatric patients.

The ChIP-exo method is used to identify protein-DNA interactions with near base pair or even single-nucleotide precision, improving upon the resolution and noise of standard ChIP-seq by incorporating lambda exonuclease digestion to footprint the borders of protein-DNA crosslink sites.

Mitochondrial DNA deletion breakpoints frequently occur within or near predicted non-B DNA conformations like hairpins, cloverleaf-like elements, and G-quadruplex forming sequences, particularly those containing intrastrand motifs with two or three consecutive guanines.

Several circular RNAs are known to be deregulated in cancers, a novel circular RNA from the IKZF3 gene is identified in B-cell malignancies, circular RNAs generally exert diverse regulatory functions important in carcinogenesis, and circular RNAs have potential as diagnostic and prognostic biomarkers in cancer.

The enzymatic activity of nardilysin is that of a metalloendopeptidase of the M16 family which promotes ectodomain shedding of the precursor forms of various growth factors and cytokines by enhancing the protease activities of ADAM proteins.
The Mantoux test detects latent Mycobacterium tuberculosis infection and tuberculosis infection in patients who do not have tuberculosis disease.
The Proseek panels measure 92 inflammation-related proteins simultaneously, or, in one instance, CVD I array measures plasma concentrations of matrix metalloproteinase-12.

No, enlimomab is not an effective treatment for ischemic stroke. Clinical trials demonstrated worse Modified Rankin Scale scores, fewer symptom-free recoveries, and increased mortality in patients treated with enlimomab compared to those receiving a placebo. Further study in rat models also showed it did not significantly reduce infarct size and could even augment it under certain conditions.

Yes, AAV vectors are considered for the treatment of retinal dystrophies. Retinal organoids can be created using AAV transfection, and optogenetic strategies utilizing AAV vectors are being investigated for the treatment of retinal degeneration and glaucoma, with preliminary results from clinical trials available. Additionally, AAV-based vectors have demonstrated potential for treating rare diseases in general.

No, the International Citicoline Trial on Acute Stroke (ICTUS) trial found that citicoline is not efficacious in the treatment of moderate-to-severe acute ischaemic stroke, as global recovery was similar in both citicoline and placebo groups. Additionally, no significant differences were reported in safety variables or adverse event rates.

Yes, human lysyl oxidase-like 2 is a glycoprotein because N-linked glycans were identified at Asn-455 and Asn-644 on the recombinant protein, and disruption of N-glycosylation inhibited secretion of the protein.

Yes, GDF15 levels are a biomarker for metformin use, and its concentration reflects the dose of metformin administered to individuals with dysglycemia. Specifically, a 1.64 ng/mL higher GDF15 level predicted a 188-mg higher metformin dose. The odds of metformin use increased with increases in GDF15 level.

No. Rituximab is considered for several off-label uses including chronic graft-versus-host disease, pemphigus vulgaris, immune thrombocytopenic purpura, cold agglutinin disease, Wegener granulomatosis, and others, but chronic fatigue syndrome is not mentioned as a condition it treats or is being investigated for within the provided information.

No, oncogene-induced DNA replication stress drives genomic instability. It is proposed that replication stress compromises DNA repair, leading to a mutator phenotype, and is a hallmark of cancer that generates genomic instability and selects for escape from apoptosis. Additionally, replication stress maps with focal genomic deletions.

Yes, the petrous bone is used in ancient DNA sampling because it yields higher amounts of endogenous DNA than other skeletal elements and is currently recognized as one of the best substrates for large-scale genomic analyses of ancient human populations. It has been used to obtain genetic material from individuals across various time periods and locations, including Anatolian Neolithic farmers and individuals from the Darra-i-Kur cave.

No, recursive splice sites are found at a much higher frequency in introns greater than 20 kb, and are underrepresented within introns less than 10 kb. This indicates recursive splicing is advantageous in the context of long introns.

Yes, lysyl oxidase and lysyl oxidase-like 2 (LOXL2) both promote crosslinking of collagen, contributing to its linearity and organization, and stabilization in tissues like the extracellular matrix and tumor tissues. LOXL2 is specifically noted as the principal isoform that crosslinks and stabilizes insoluble collagen deposition.

Yes, sternotomy closure is performed using either a ZipFix™ system or conventional steel wire following cardiac surgery. The ZipFix™ system, consisting of biocompatible poly-ether-ether-ketone cable-ties, is presented as an alternative to traditional wire closure, and studies compare outcomes between patients closed with ZipFix™ and those closed with conventional wires. Multifilament cable wiring is also compared to conventional steel wires for sternal closure.

Yes, eRNAs are transcribed bidirectionally from active regulatory sequences, and some enhancers encode two bidirectional nonpolyadenylated RNAs. Additionally, studies detect eRNA transcription sites with high sensitivity and specificity using techniques that capture bidirectional transcription initiation.

Yes, pelota is a ribosomal rescue factor. Loss of GTPBP2, a binding partner of pelota, causes ribosome stalling, and the results define GTPBP2 as a ribosome rescue factor, suggesting a role for pelota in this process. Additionally, pelota, along with Hbs1, is responsible for solving general problems of ribosomal stall in translation.

Yes, CXCL7 is a chemokine as it is repeatedly referred to as such and described as mediating biological processes, interacting with receptors, and being involved in various pathophysiological processes like thrombosis and cancer invasion. It is also stated to be highly expressed in platelets and to bind heparin and the CXCR2 receptor.

No. The provided information details the role of TRPM2 in Alzheimer’s disease, myocardial ischemia/reperfusion injury, stroke, and neuronal senescence, but there is no mention of thromboembolism or conditions directly related to its development. The contexts focus on oxidative stress, calcium dysregulation, immune cell activity, and neuronal toxicity, none of which directly implicate spontaneous thromboembolism.

Yes, a single serine-to-asparagine substitution in the prM protein of Zika virus contributes to fetal microcephaly, as contemporary epidemic strains have accumulated this mutation from their Asian ancestor. Additionally, studies link maternal Zika virus infection to congenital microcephaly and an increase in birth defects, with the ZIKV genome found in tissues of infants with microcephaly.

The aim of the PhenCode database is to help understand phenotypes of human mutations by connecting clinical data with genome sequence and functional data. It connects human phenotype and clinical data in locus-specific databases with information from projects like ENCODE and the UCSC Genome Browser. Users can explore mutations and phenotypes, viewing complementary information such as functional data and evolutionary history. Ultimately, the project plans to include genomewide data to aid in understanding the relationship between genetic variations and observable traits.

The Sp3 procedure is a novel protocol utilizing paramagnetic beads for proteomic sample preparation. It’s a rapid and unbiased method performed in a single tube, enhancing analysis, especially when working with limited sample quantities. This technique, also known as Single-Pot Solid-Phase-enhanced Sample Preparation, improves efficiency, scalability, speed, throughput, and flexibility in profiling proteomes.

Enhancer hijacking is a mechanism where enhancers inappropriately activate genes, contributing to oncogene expression and cancer development. This can occur through chromosomal alterations and increased deposition of acetylated histone H3 lysine 27, leading to altered chromatin conformation and interactions. In some cases, enhancer hijacking involves translocations or rearrangements that create new interactions between enhancers and oncogenes, or the formation of new 3D contact domains, ultimately resulting in dysregulation of gene expression. It can also involve enhancers serving as donors in translocations, or neoenhancer formation at oncogene loci.

Tinea is a dermatophyte infection of the skin, classified by the site it affects. These infections are common at all ages and have a worldwide distribution, including variations in clinical presentation that can mimic other skin diseases. Several types exist, such as tinea capitis, tinea cruris, tinea pedis, tinea corporis, tinea versicolor, and onychomycosis, and treatment involves both oral and topical antifungal agents.

Deletion breakpoints can be determined at the nucleotide level using techniques like fluorescence in situ hybridization, quantitative real-time polymerase chain reaction, and Southern blot hybridization.
