Ipilimumab, L612, and IgG1-iS18 are monoclonal antibody drugs used to treat late stage melanoma, with ipilimumab showing effectiveness in some patients and L612 demonstrating antitumor effects, while IgG1-iS18 is a promising therapeutic strategy for both early and late stages.
The transcription factor Suppressor of Hairless activates the transcription of Enhancer of Split genes in Drosophila melanogaster.

No, tocilizumab is a humanized anti-human IL-6 receptor antibody and acts as an interleukin (IL)-6 receptor antagonist. It is used in patients with an inadequate response to, or intolerance of, prior tumor necrosis factor (TNF) antagonist therapy, suggesting it is distinct from TNF inhibitors.

KIF1A-related disorders in children encompass a wide range of central and peripheral nervous system involvement, varying in severity. Some patients exhibit severe phenotypes including congenital contractures, spasticity, and respiratory insufficiency, while others present with movement disorders, subtle dysautonomia, and learning difficulties. Mutations in the KIF1A gene are linked to spastic paraplegia and the nature of the mutation can predict the underlying phenotype, with epilepsy being common in more severely affected children. White matter changes on brain MRI are also frequently observed, and can be subtle or progressive.

Pitolisant functions as an antagonist/inverse agonist of the histamine H3 autoreceptor, offering a novel and distinctive mechanism compared to other narcolepsy therapies. It is a non-imidazole based compound and does not enhance plasma cocaine concentrations by blocking cytochrome P450 activity, unlike thioperamide. Additionally, pitolisant selectively inhibits calcium increases in peripheral sensory neurons, potentially impacting TRPV1 activation at high concentrations.

Endoplasmic reticulum stress is an imbalance between protein folding load and capacity, leading to the accumulation of unfolded proteins within the endoplasmic reticulum. It is associated with the pathophysiology of various liver diseases and can mediate the accumulation of abnormal proteins, oxidative stress, and apoptosis in hepatocytes. This stress has also been implicated in conditions like depression, rheumatoid arthritis, and can contribute to inflammatory and metabolic diseases.

An operon is a cluster of genes, often adjacent, that share a common promoter and are transcribed into a single mRNA molecule. These genes typically encode proteins with related roles, providing an efficient way to coordinate their expression. Operons can vary in size, containing up to eight genes, and some may even include genes transcribed in opposite directions—a structure known as a noncontiguous operon. They are found in both bacterial and eukaryote genomes, and their evolution involves gains and losses over time.

No, circular RNAs appear to regulate gene expression, rather than increase it. The texts describe circRNAs as having “functional effects on gene expression” and serving as “sponges for micro RNAs and RNA binding proteins” and “acting as transcriptional regulators,” indicating a regulatory role, not a simple increase in expression. Additionally, perturbations in circRNA expression are associated with disease, further suggesting a role in gene regulation and cellular homeostasis.

CMB305 is a therapeutic vaccine regimen containing an integration-deficient, dendritic-cell targeting lentiviral vector called LV305 to prime NY-ESO-1 specific T cells, as well as a boost from a NY-ESO-1 protein vaccine given alongside a toll-like-4 receptor agonist, glycopyranosyl lipid A.
Biallelic mutations in G-Protein coupled receptor kinase 1 (GRK1) cause GRK1 associated Oguchi disease, a rare subtype of congenital stationary night blindness.

No, adjuvant treatment with the MAGE-A3 immunotherapeutic did not increase disease-free survival compared with placebo in patients with MAGE-A3-positive surgically resected non-small-cell lung cancer, and further development of the immunotherapeutic for use in NSCLC has been stopped.

Yes, ALEX1 contains two armadillo repeat domains and is a novel member of the Armadillo family. It is described as having two Armadillo repeats as opposed to the more than six repeats found in classical Armadillo family members.
Anticitrullinated peptide antibodies (ACPAs) are associated with rheumatoid arthritis (RA).

Emergency granulopoiesis is a component of the innate immune response that is induced by infectious or inflammatory challenges. It involves the rapid expansion and differentiation of granulocyte/monocyte progenitor populations, contributing to host defense during infections. This process requires both differentiation and proliferation of granulocyte precursors and is mediated by factors like G-CSF to rapidly mobilize bone marrow granulocyte progenitors. Ultimately, emergency granulopoiesis results in increased neutrophil production to combat pathogens.
G1P[8] was the dominant rotavirus genotype nationally in Australia before vaccine introduction.
Missense mutations in FHF2/FGF13 and GRIA3 cause X-linked developmental and epileptic encephalopathy.

Yes, radiotherapy for cervical cancer increases the risk of colon cancer. Analyses show the risk of colon cancer diverged approximately 8 years after initial radiation treatment, with a hazard ratio of 2.00 for women with radiation versus those without. The absolute risk of developing colon cancer after 35 years was 6.5% for those who received radiation, compared to 2.5% for those who did not.
Human antibody isotypes include immunoglobulin A, immunoglobulin D, immunoglobulin E, immunoglobulin G, immunoglobulin M, IgG1, IgG2, IgG3, and IgG4.

Central retinal artery occlusion (CRAO) is an ocular emergency that causes acute, painless visual loss in the affected eye. It’s an uncommon eye disorder resulting from ischemic stroke of the eye and causes cell death in the inner retinal layers, though the outer layers may be spared. Vision loss from CRAO can be severe and irreversible, but supplemental oxygen or treatments like rt-PA may restore vision if administered quickly enough.

Yes, fbp1 is affected by glucose starvation stress. Glucose starvation triggers lncRNA transcription across the fbp1 promoter, promoting chromatin remodeling and fbp1 mRNA expression. Additionally, fbp1 expression is induced upon glucose starvation, and the cell integrity MAPK pathway and its effectors play a critical role in adapting to grow on alternative carbon sources by inducing fbp1+ gene expression.
csDMARD stands for conventional synthetic disease-modifying antirheumatic drugs.

Loss of Scube2 causes defective endochondral bone formation and impairs Ihh-mediated chondrocyte differentiation and proliferation, as well as osteoblast differentiation of bone-marrow mesenchymal stromal-cell cultures.

No. Semagacestat failed to slow disease progression in Phase III studies and was associated with worsening of clinical measures of cognition and the ability to perform activities of daily living. Development of the drug was halted after preliminary results showed it did not provide therapeutic benefit and carried risks like increased skin cancer incidence. While it lowers Aβ levels, clinical efficacy has not been detectable to date.

Histone deacetylases, a family of enzymes including sirtuins, remove acetyl groups from histone tails, while UTX and JMJD3 specifically remove methyl marks on H3K27, and various deubiquitinases remove ubiquitin modifications from histones.
The human mitochondrial genome contains approximately 16,569 base pairs, and is also described as being around 16.5 kilobase pairs or 16 kB in length.

Yes, some nematodes contain a CTCF gene. While it has been lost in derived nematodes like *C. elegans*, orthologs of CTCF are present in a subset of nematodes, specifically basal nematodes, and a CTCF-like gene was cloned from *Trichinella spiralis*.

No, tocilizumab is not a csDMARD. The text indicates that first-line treatment for rheumatoid arthritis is based on “slow-acting” antirheumatic agents, generally methotrexate, which are csDMARDs. Tocilizumab is described as an option *after* failure of these initial drugs, and is categorized as a bDMARD/tsDMARD alongside abatacept and rituximab.
Heterozygous lamin B1 and lamin B2 variants cause primary microcephaly and define a novel laminopathy.

The risk of secondary cancers after proton beam therapy is low but not negligible. Compared to photons, the risk of secondary cancer induction can be reduced by a factor of three due to the reduction of integral dose. Simulations suggest that both passively scattered and scanned-beam proton therapies confer significantly lower risks of cancer induction than intensity-modulated radiation therapy or conventional photon treatments.
Essential aminoacids include valine, leucine, isoleucine, phenylalanine, methionine, lysine, tryptophan, and cysteine.
Retapamulin treats primary impetigo, skin infections such as impetigo, and secondarily infected dermatitis caused by Staphylococcus aureus and Streptococcus pyogenes.

Histone H3K27ac distinguishes active enhancers from inactive/poised enhancer elements containing H3K4me1 alone, and acetylation of lysine residues in the globular domain of histone H3, such as H3K122ac, marks a new class of active enhancers that lack H3K27ac.
DMARD stands for disease-modifying antirheumatic drug.

Yes, pathogenic SMAD6 variants substantially increase the risk of both nonsyndromic and syndromic presentations of craniosynostosis, particularly metopic synostosis. Loss-of-function variants of SMAD6 are enriched in individuals with craniosynostosis compared to general population data.
Pexidartinib targets the Colony-Stimulating Factor (CSF1)/CSF1 receptor pathway and inhibits the CSF1 receptor.

The microgenderome defines the interaction between microbiota, sex hormones and the immune system, and it highlights the role of sex differences in the host-microbiota interaction relevant for conditions like autoimmune and neuro-immune disorders.

Jaundice, or the yellowing of skin and mucous membranes, is caused by hyperbilirubinemia. In patients with liver failure, this can be due to hepatic metastases, often seen in gastrointestinal malignancies like gastric or colorectal cancer. Additionally, jaundice can result from disorders of bilirubin metabolism and hepatocellular dysfunction, which are underlying mechanisms of cholestasis. Biliary tract obstruction from tumors or metastases can also lead to jaundice and subsequent liver failure.

Yes, Tcf3 is a terminal component of the canonical Wnt pathway in embryonic stem cells and interacts with β-catenin. It also appears to be involved in both activating and repressing Wnt-β-catenin signaling depending on the cellular context, functioning as an integral component of the core regulatory circuitry and influencing the balance between pluripotency and differentiation. Additionally, Wnt5a and Wnt16b signaling are linked to TCF3-PBX1 expression.

Yes, the introduction of rotavirus vaccines has altered the predominant genotypes. Before vaccine introduction, G1P[8] was dominant, but afterward, greater genotype diversity was observed with fluctuating dominance, and different vaccines were associated with different dominant genotypes – G12P[8] with RotaTeq and equine-like G3P[8] and G2P[4] with Rotarix. Emerging genotypes like G12[P6] and G12[P8] have also been noted.
Joubert syndrome is caused by dysfunction of the ciliary ARMC9/TOGARAM1 protein module.

Yes, epidemiological studies consistently show increased tumor incidence in pediatric patients exposed to head CTs, with an excess relative risk of 1.29 for those exposed to one or more scans. Even a single CT exam appears to carry a small excess risk, and tumor incidence increases with the number of scans received. However, one study did not show an increased risk, and further refined methods are needed to control for confounding factors.
Remdesivir inhibits viral replication by targeting the viral polymerase and the proofreading exoribonuclease.

Pleuromutilin, a natural tricyclic compound, can be isolated from the fungus *Pleurotus mutilus*. Production of pleuromutilin can be enhanced using a specific dissolved oxygen control strategy, reaching up to 12g/L.  Pleuromutilin also exhibits antibacterial properties and can inhibit the proliferation and migration of certain ovarian carcinoma cells.

Otx2 is associated with primed pluripotency, as it is upregulated during the transition from naive pluripotency and recruits Oct4 to a different set of enhancers, inducing the primed pluripotency gene network.

No, AGY is described as a novel egg yolk-derived anti-gliadin antibody used to neutralize gluten in patients with celiac disease and may improve the efficacy of a gluten-free diet; it is not presented as a replacement therapy for any other condition. Enzyme replacement therapy is mentioned as a treatment for Gaucher disease, but AGY is not identified as such.

De novo SOX6 variants cause a neurodevelopmental syndrome. This syndrome is associated with developmental delay and/or intellectual disability, and can also include attention-deficit/hyperactivity disorder (ADHD), autism, craniosynostosis, and osteochondromas. These variants are predicted to inactivate the SOX6 allele, and are not detected in control cohorts.

Yes, irradiation acts as an oncogenic factor and can lead to the development of glioblastoma multiforme, sometimes developing during remission from other cancers like acute lymphocytic leukemia. Several cases report glioblastoma occurring years after cranial irradiation and chemotherapy, and patients treated with irradiation carry a risk of developing secondary cranial neoplasms many years later.

Yes, aggrephagy is described as the selective degradation of protein aggregates by macroautophagy, and is a selective branch of autophagy that deals with the identification, capture, and degradation of protein aggregates. It is also referred to as the aggresome-macroautophagy pathway.
Blood group antigens include A, B, H type 2, Lex, sialylated Lex, Lewis(a), Lewis(b), Lewis(x), and Lewis(y), with type A and type B antigens being specifically Neu5Gc and Neu5Ac in cats.
The murine beta globin locus region is marked by six DNaseI hypersensitive sites, though studies have focused on five major sites and their individual contributions to gene expression.

Tocilizumab was studied to determine its effect on relapse rates in patients with giant-cell arteritis during glucocorticoid tapering. In a year-long trial, sustained remission at week 52 occurred in 56% of patients treated with tocilizumab weekly and 53% of those treated every other week. This was compared to a 14% remission rate in the placebo group undergoing a 26-week prednisone taper.
The de novo heterozygous predicted deleterious missense variant c.G2873A (p.R958H) in ABCC8 is associated with idiopathic Pulmonary Arterial Hypertension (PAH).
The Fahn-Tolosa-Marin scale is used to rate tremor in conditions such as essential tremor and parkinsonism.
The core SNARE complex proteins are VAMP2, Syntaxin, and SNAP25, with synaptobrevin, SNAP-25, and syntaxin also being involved in neurotransmitter release and membrane fusion events.

Septicemia is a generalized bacterial infection with a persistent bacteremia. It can occur with endocarditis and is associated with a systemic inflammatory response syndrome (SIRS) or septic shock. Certain bacteria, like *Fusobacterium nucleatum* and *Capnocytophaga canimorsus*, can cause septicemic infections, sometimes linked to animal contact or underlying health conditions like alcohol abuse.
TAL1 is a master oncogenic transcription factor in T-cell acute lymphoblastic leukemia, abnormally expressed due to the replacement of regulator elements and crucial for leukemogenesis.

European influenza vaccines include Vaxigrip, Begrivac, Influsplit/Fluarix, Influvac, Agrippal, Fluvirin, a trivalent attenuated nasal influenza vaccine (Fluenz), and a trivalent MF59 adjuvated inactivated influenza vaccine (Fluad), as well as seasonal, inactivated trivalent, quadrivalent, cellular, or recombinant vaccines.

canSAR is a public, integrative knowledgebase focused on cancer translational research and drug discovery. It integrates various types of data—biological, pharmacological, chemical, structural, genomic, protein, and druggability—into a single information portal. Researchers can use canSAR to access information about proteins, cancers, drugs, and cell lines to help solve bottlenecks in cancer translation and drug discovery, and it is widely used by both academic and industry researchers internationally. It is accessible at http://cansar.icr.ac.uk.

The provided information does not contain details regarding the mechanism of action of magrolimab. However, several mechanisms of action are discussed within the provided text, including gene delivery inhibiting phenotype progression, non-genomic action initiating hormonal effects that interface with genomic effects, and blocking the renin-angiotensin-aldosterone system at a rate-limiting step. These examples demonstrate various ways biological processes can be targeted and influenced.
Mutations include those that sustain in one specific tumor type, rare driver mutations, clustered mutations in multiple sequence alignments, hotspot mutations, and those harboring conserved residues.
HER-2 belongs to the family of tyrosine kinase transmembrane proteins and the human epidermal growth factor receptor family.

Etanercept is a fusion protein that competitively inhibits tumor necrosis factor (TNF). It consists of the receptor (CD120b) for TNF linked to the Fc portion of IgG1, or a fully human TNFRII ectodomain linked to the Fc portion of human IgG1. The protein also possesses N- and O-glycosylation sites, indicating a complex structure with both carbohydrate and protein components.
bDMARD stands for biologic disease-modifying anti-rheumatic drugs, and targeted-synthetic DMARDs are also used in treatment.

Yes, pks+ Escherichia coli promotes carcinogenesis and facilitates colorectal cancer progression through the production of colibactin, a genotoxin that induces double-strand DNA breaks. Additionally, a mutational signature in colorectal cancer is caused by genotoxic pks.

No, tirilazad increases death and disability by about one fifth when given to patients with acute ischemic stroke and has no overall effect on infarct volume. Further trials of tirilazad are unwarranted.
The major families of histones include H1, H2A, H2B, H3, and H4, and these are generally consistent across eukaryotic plant and animal species.

Yes, the genome of paramyxoviruses is a single strand of negative-sense RNA and is non-segmented. Several sources describe the paramyxovirus family as having a genome consisting of a single strand of negative sense RNA, and one specifically states the genomes are non-segmented. Additionally, Beilong virus, a novel paramyxovirus, has a non-segmented negative-stranded RNA genome.
Seven hundred and eighty-three non-pseudoautosomal region (PAR) X-chromosome genes are mentioned in relation to tumor-suppressor genes.

No, exposure to bDMARDs during pregnancy does not seem to interfere with post-natal development up to infancy. Further studies are needed to confirm that bDMARDs do not have a negative impact on psychomotor achievements in newborns.

No, KMT2D missense variants located in a specific region spanning exons 38 and 39 cause a multiple malformations syndrome distinct from KS1. Individuals with these variants exhibit clinical features and genome-wide DNA methylation patterns significantly different from those with KS1.
The NoSAS score can be used for screening of sleep-disordered breathing, mild sleep-disordered breathing, moderate-to-severe sleep-disordered breathing, and severe sleep-disordered breathing.

Klinefelter syndrome is a chromosomal abnormality characterized by a 47, XXY karyotype, often resulting from meiotic chromosomal nondisjunction, and sometimes associated with translocations like 13/14.

Repeat Insertion Domains of LncRNAs (RIDLs) are exonic transposable elements that act as RNA, DNA, and protein-binding domains within long noncoding RNAs, essentially being repurposed as recognition sites for both protein and nucleic acids.
FTY720 has been approved by the FDA as the first oral drug for the treatment of multiple sclerosis.
Fingolimod is a drug that becomes active following phosphorylation into fingolimod-phosphate.
Mutations in HFE cause hereditary hemochromatosis, a primary cause of iron overload, and classical hereditary hemochromatosis type 1 is specifically caused by bi-allelic mutations of this gene.

Operons are frequently found in the *C. elegans* genome, comprising approximately 15% of its protein-coding genes. Almost all genes within these operons are expressed in germline tissue, suggesting a link between germline expression and operon organization. Mechanisms governing germline gene expression appear to influence both the origin and maintenance of operons, indicating that tissue-specific gene expression can significantly impact genome organization.
The TLR7 locus is encoded by an X chromosome locus in the human genome.
DNaseI hypersensitive sites mark cis-regulatory elements, genomic regulatory regions, and open chromatin structures that correlate with transcriptional activity.

Yes, polycomb group proteins are involved in forming chromatin loops. Several sources indicate PcG proteins bind the genome and form chromatin domains, including higher-order chromatin structures involving interactions between PcG-bound regions at various distances. Furthermore, repressive loops within polycomb domains are formed by the action of polycomb proteins, and preventing loop formation decreases silencing efficiency even when polycomb components are present.
DESeq2 performs differential analysis of count data, such as read counts per gene in RNA-seq, for evidence of systematic changes across experimental conditions.

A protein degron is an amino acid sequence that encodes a signal for protein degradation, often involving a poly-ubiquitin chain. These signals induce rapid proteolysis by the proteasome, effectively controlling protein abundance within cells. Degrons can be utilized to target proteins for degradation, offering a tunable methodology for controlling cellular function and providing a tool for biomedical research. Some degrons can even be artificially induced or conditionally activated to deplete specific proteins of interest.

Synteny is defined using methods such as OrthoCluster, MCScanX algorithm, BLAST similarity information, FFAdj-MCS which assesses pairwise gene order using adjacencies, and the uniform Double-Cut-and-Join model to model the evolution of synteny blocks.

Yes, ATAC-seq can be employed in single-cell mode, as evidenced by the development and application of techniques like single-cell ATAC-seq (scATAC-seq) and single-cell combinatorial indexing ATAC-seq (sci-ATAC-seq) which detect open chromatin in individual cells and profile the chromatin accessibility landscape at a single-cell level. Several tools, such as SCALE and Scasat, have also been created specifically for analyzing scATAC-seq data.

B-cells can transdifferentiate into macrophages through the expression of transcription factors, specifically C/EBPα and C/EBPβ, which rapidly and efficiently reprogram the cells. This process involves inhibiting the B cell commitment transcription factor Pax5 and synergizing with PU.1 to upregulate myeloid markers.  Importantly, this conversion can occur at high frequencies and doesn’t necessarily involve reverting to earlier progenitor states.

ChromHMM annotates the noncoding genome using epigenomic information from one or multiple cell types. It combines genome-wide epigenomic maps and uses patterns of marks to infer a complete annotation for each cell type. The software learns chromatin-state signatures and generates a genome-wide annotation, also providing automated enrichment analysis for functional interpretation of each state. Ultimately, ChromHMM helps to identify relationships between DNA sequences and chromatin states.

FeatureCounts is a read summarization program used for counting reads generated from either RNA or genomic DNA sequencing experiments to determine the number of reads mapping to each genomic feature like exons or genes.

Otx2 and Nanog exhibit mutual antagonism, specifying different cell fates during development. Otx2 generally drives cells towards a rostral fate or a primed pluripotency state, while Nanog antagonizes Otx2 and promotes caudal fate or naive pluripotency. This antagonism specifies a spectrum of identities in embryonic stem cells and is critical for early neural development and lineage specification.

Yes, a new class of super-enhancers is associated with fast recovery of 3D chromatin loops, and they exhibit high levels of local chromatin interactions and a higher order of local chromatin organization. Additionally, super-lncRNAs contribute to the local chromatin organization of super-enhancers.

Yes, IL18 signaling promotes the homing of mature Tregs into the thymus, and a subset of thymus recirculating IL18R cells exists. Additionally, studies discuss T cell development within the thymus and factors influencing it.

No. Several instances demonstrate antiphlogistics *decreasing* inflammation or edema, such as Ibuprofen reducing inflammatory edema and olipiphate exhibiting anti-inflammatory and reparative effects on arthritis and wounds. Additionally, studies investigated whether antiphlogistics might *overestimate* SPF by suppressing inflammatory responses, implying they work against inflammation, not promote it.
Rilonacept targets interleukin-1α and interleukin-1β, and it also acts as an interleukin-1 blocker and traps IL-1β through blockade of the IL-1 receptor.
DeepCC is a novel deep learning-based framework developed for cancer molecular subtype classification.
Antidepressant drugs have been given a black box warning regarding the increased risk of suicide in children, adolescents, and young adults.
Carpel tunnel syndrome affects the median nerve, leading to compression and resulting in pain, numbness, and functional dysfunction of the hand.
Anakinra is a recombinant human interleukin-1 receptor antagonist approved for the treatment of rheumatoid arthritis.
Herpes viruses have a large double-stranded DNA genome.

Yes, liraglutide is effective for weight reduction. Studies show it reduces body weight, with higher doses demonstrating better efficacy, and can lead to overt weight reduction as defined by a decrease of >5% within 12 weeks. Long-term treatment effectively maintains this reduction, and it is associated with weight loss in clinical trials lasting up to a year. It works, in part, by acting on neurons within the arcuate nucleus of the brain.

Current evidence does not confirm an increased risk of cancer with lorcaserin, though a trend in that direction is suggested. One trial indicated a higher risk of lung and pancreatic cancer in the lorcaserin group, but not colon cancer. Applications for marketing authorization for lorcaserin were withdrawn due to a negative benefit/risk balance, and long-term trials assessing its impact on mortality and morbidity are unavailable.

No, the incidence of disease progression was not significantly lower with the combination of eflornithine and sulindac than with either drug alone in a trial involving patients with familial adenomatous polyposis. Hazard ratios comparing the combination therapy to sulindac and eflornithine alone were 0.71 and 0.66, respectively, with confidence intervals that included 1, indicating no significant difference.
Darolutamide is a medicine used to treat men with nonmetastatic prostate cancer, and nonmetastatic castration-resistant prostate cancer.

Selpercatinib can be used to treat lung cancers with RET fusions or mutations, metastatic progressive RET-mutant medullary thyroid carcinoma, other RET-driven cancers, and advanced medullary thyroid cancer with RET mutations or fusions.
Volanesorsen targets apolipoprotein C-III synthesis, inhibiting its translation via an antisense oligonucleotide mechanism to lower plasma levels of Apo-CIII and triglycerides.
Roflumilast cream is effective for the treatment of chronic plaque psoriasis.

Givosiran is a small interfering RNA (siRNA) that triggers RNA interference to silence the gene for delta-aminolevulinic acid synthase 1 (ALAS1). By downregulating ALAS1 mRNA, givosiran prevents the buildup of neurotoxic delta-aminolevulinic acid and porphobilinogen, which are associated with acute porphyria attacks. It utilizes a delivery system involving N-acetylgalactosamine to specifically target and enter hepatic cells, ultimately reducing ALA and PBG metabolites to normal physiological levels.

Yes, olaparib demonstrates effectiveness in treating prostate cancer, particularly metastatic castration-recurrent prostate cancer and advanced castration-resistant prostate cancer. Clinical trials have shown high response rates, nearly 90% in some cases, and improvements in progression-free survival for patients with specific genetic mutations or when combined with other therapies. It is also being investigated as a maintenance therapy and in combination with first-line endocrine treatments.

Corkscrew esophagus is a finding in barium studies and upper gastrointestinal endoscopy characterized by twisted segments resembling a corkscrew or rosary beads. It reflects abnormal contractions and compartmentalization of the esophagus, and is often associated with esophageal motility disorders like diffuse esophageal spasm or, less commonly, jackhammer esophagus. While typically linked to distal esophageal spasm, it can also appear with hypercontractile esophagus, and is sometimes demonstrated endoscopically as a radiological diagnosis.
Methotrexate, sulfasalazine, and leflunomide are three conventional synthetic DMARDs used in the management of rheumatoid arthritis.

Yes, isradipine shows potential as a neuroprotective agent for Parkinson’s disease, particularly in early stages, as it has demonstrated neuroprotective effects in animal models and, in re-analyses of clinical trials, has been associated with a delay in the need for symptomatic medication and lower medication burden. However, one phase 3 clinical trial initially failed to show efficacy, though subsequent examination of the data suggested benefits with longer exposure.
Yes, MK-1602 is a small-molecule CGRP receptor antagonist currently in phase 3 studies for the acute treatment of migraine.
Lundbeck Seattle BioPharmaceuticals developed eptinezumab for the prevention of migraine.

AESOP syndrome is a rare condition characterized by adenopathy, an extensive skin patch overlying a plasmacytoma, and can present with unusual histologic findings like dermal vascular proliferation alongside conspicuous fibrohistiocytic cells, and is often associated with an underlying plasma cell dyscrasia, sometimes coexisting with POEMS syndrome which includes polyneuropathy, organomegaly, endocrinopathy, monoclonal protein, and skin changes.
The ViFuP regimen for breast cancer includes vinorelbine, cisplatin, and 5-fluorouracil administered as a continuous infusion.
The EE-4A regimen includes dactinomycin and vincristine.

Yes, inactivation of CYLD exacerbates colitis-associated colorectal carcinogenesis, as the number of adenomas dramatically increased in mice with inactivated CYLD when challenged with genotoxic and pro-inflammatory agents. Functional intestinal epithelial CYLD plays a critical tumor-suppressing role in this process.

metPropagate, a network-based approach utilizing label propagation, can be used to prioritize candidate genes in patients with suspected inborn errors of metabolism by considering each gene's metabolic perturbation and the network of interactions between neighbors.
The IROX regimen for colorectal cancer includes irinotecan and oxaliplatin, administered at dosages of 200 mg/m² and 85 mg/m², respectively, every three weeks.

Pemigatinib selectively and potently inhibits fibroblast growth factor receptor 1, 2, and 3, and it is being investigated for its ability to address FGFR-driven malignancies as well as resistance to therapies targeting other tyrosine-kinase receptors.

Yes, results indicate high levels of nucleotide diversity in this species, and analysis of genes in Drosophila melanogaster suggests that late-replicating regions have higher divergence and diversity.

ncPro-ML is a new method for identifying ncRNA promoters, while the Eukaryotic Promoter Database (EPD) provides accurate transcription start site information for promoters—including certain classes of ncRNAs from human and mouse—and offers enhanced data visualization through improved promoter viewers and customized views combining EPD tracks with UCSC Genome Browser tracks.
AZD8601 is administered via intradermal injection, either as a single dose or through sequential dosing, formulated in a biocompatible vehicle.

SAR425899 is a novel polypeptide that functions as an agonist at both the glucagon-like peptide-1 receptor and the glucagon receptor. It is a dual glucagon-like peptide-1 receptor/glucagon receptor agonist being investigated for the treatment of type 2 diabetes. Treatment with SAR425899 improves postprandial glucose control, enhances β-cell function, and leads to weight reduction in both healthy volunteers and patients with type 2 diabetes.
Neurodevelopmental disorders are caused by AMPA receptor GluA2 subunit defects.

Yes, Hunter disease is associated with the X chromosome. The provided information details localization of the gene for Hunter’s syndrome to the distal region of the X chromosome, describes the disease as X-linked recessive, and discusses skewed X chromosome inactivation patterns in affected females, all indicating a connection to the X chromosome.

Integrins are transmembrane receptors localized in the cell membrane and can also be found internalized within endosomes, and they interact with both the extracellular matrix and the cell interior to regulate cellular processes.
A bacteriocin is a ribosomally produced antimicrobial peptide used by diverse bacteria to compete against closely related species, and can also be a narrow-spectrum protein antibiotic.
Coffin-Siris syndrome is associated with alterations in the BAF complex, also known as the mammalian SWI/SNF complex, which contributes to chromatin remodeling.

Aortitis is an inflammatory process involving one or more layers of the aortic wall that can be caused by infectious or noninfectious diseases, frequently resulting in aortic root dilatation and aortic insufficiency.

TAFRO syndrome consists of thrombocytopenia, anasarca, fever, reticulin fibrosis, and organomegaly, and is considered a unique variant or subtype of idiopathic multicentric Castleman disease alongside iMCD-not otherwise specified.

Curli is a component of bacterial biofilms, specifically forming amyloid fibrils secreted by the curli biogenesis system. It is a substrate recognized by CsgG, with specific recognition sites for its N-terminus located in crevices outside of the CsgG channel. Understanding curli biogenesis is important for developing treatments for biofilm-related infections, and the CsgF-CsgG complex plays a role in its secretion.
The core matrisome comprises approximately 300 proteins in mammals, and in lung tissue specifically, 104 proteins are considered core matrisome proteins that make up the extracellular matrix.

Yes, arthritis can be experimentally caused in rats by subcutaneously injecting Freund's complete adjuvant into the subplantar area of the left hind paw, and this method is used to establish an RA model in multiple studies.

Yes, saponins can be used as adjuvants. Several studies detail the design, synthesis, and evaluation of saponin variants as promising vaccine adjuvants, noting their immunostimulatory activities and potential to potentiate immune responses. Additionally, they can be used as a cancer immunotherapeutic adjuvant.

No, erabutoxin b is found in the venom of sea snakes, specifically Laticauda semifasciata, and is a neurotoxin that inhibits the acetylcholine receptor. It is described as a protein isolated from these snakes and is compared to toxins from other snakes, not plants.

Yes, Toll-like receptors are essential in host defense against pathogens by virtue of their capacity to detect microbes and initiate the immune response. They recognize conserved pathogen molecular patterns and induce an inflammatory response, and are activated by bacteria like *Burkholderia pseudomallei* and *Salmonella*.
A decoy exosome displays tumor antigens that induce autoantibodies and inhibits complement-mediated cytotoxicity towards cancer cells.

Myasthenia gravis is a chronic neuromuscular autoimmune disease characterized by muscular weakness and fatigability that worsens with activity and improves with rest. It occurs when antibodies interfere with muscular contraction at the neuromuscular junction, commonly targeting acetylcholine receptors but sometimes targeting muscle-specific kinase (MuSK) protein. While not completely curable, the condition can be managed to allow for a relatively high quality of life, and treatments like plasma exchange and immunosuppressive therapies have shown promising results, with thymectomy being a viable option in some cases. It can be challenging to diagnose, particularly the MuSK-Ab subtype, and some patients do not respond well to conventional steroid therapy.
Hydrometrocolpos, polydactyly, and congenital heart disease are three disorders commonly associated with McKusick-Kaufman syndrome.

Yes, Ixodes is a genus of tick containing many species, including Ixodes ricinus, Ixodes persulcatus, Ixodes hexagonus, Ixodes auritulus, Ixodes howelli, Ixodes signatus, and Ixodes uriae, with over 240 species identified. Several documents discuss characteristics and identification of ticks within the Ixodes genus.

The most common hand sanitizers contain either ethanol or isopropanol as the active ingredient, with concentrations ranging from 60% to 95% alcohol, and some utilize 0.12% benzalkonium chloride as an alternative active ingredient.
Fingolimod was the first oral drug for the treatment of multiple sclerosis approved by the US Food and Drug Administration in 2010.

Fingolimod is a non-selective agonist of all S1P receptors with the exception of one. It modulates S1P receptors, impacting cells within the central nervous system and influencing remyelination through S1P3 and S1P5 receptors. Additionally, fingolimod impacts the heart via S1P receptor subtype 1 and inhibits hERG current, potentially prolonging the QT interval.

Fingolimod is a synthetic compound based on the fungal secondary metabolite myriocin, also known as ISP-I. It was synthesized while researchers investigated structure-activity relationships of derivatives of ISP-I, which was isolated from *Isaria sinclairii*. The synthesis of fingolimod occurred at Kyoto University in 1992 by the research group of Tetsuro Fujita.

The provided information does not contain any details regarding fingolimod or its effects on brain grey matter. However, alterations in grey matter volume are associated with conditions like depression, holoprosencephaly, SCA3, and primary blepharospasm. These alterations can manifest as increases or decreases in specific brain regions, suggesting grey matter is dynamically affected by various neurological processes.
During the FREEDOMS study, patients were assigned to receive either 0.5 mg or 1.25 mg of fingolimod once daily, or a placebo, for 24 months.
The FREEDOMS study included 1272 patients with relapsing-remitting multiple sclerosis.

Blepharospasm is a distressing focal dystonia characterized by involuntary contractions of the orbicularis oculi muscles, often presenting as bilateral, synchronous, and symmetric involuntary eyelid closures or spasms.

Balovaptan is an investigational vasopressin 1a receptor antagonist being evaluated to improve social communication and interaction. It selectively blocks the V1a receptor in the brain and has shown improvement in surrogates of social behavior and adaptive behaviors in individuals with autism spectrum disorder. Its pharmacokinetic properties are significantly altered by strong modulators of CYP3A4 activity, meaning other drugs can impact how the body processes it.

Bisphenol A is an industrial chemical that can interfere with the endocrine system and is found ubiquitously in the environment and the human body. Exposure to Bisphenol A has been associated with adverse health effects, including reproductive abnormalities, obesity, and even cancer. It can induce oxidative stress, reduce sperm count and motility, and down-regulate the cannabinoid receptor 1, potentially leading to reduced food intake and fat mass. Bisphenol A is rapidly processed and excreted by humans, though species differences exist in how it is metabolized and eliminated from the body.

Human saliva contains amylases, cystatins, immunoglobulins, myosin heavy chain, phosphatidylethanolamine binding protein, secretory actin-binding protein precursor, triosephosphate isomerase, and annexin-A1 protein.

No, HbA1c is not a perfect biomarker for well-controlled diabetes. Common comorbidities and certain medications can compromise its sensitivity and specificity, suggesting it has limitations in accurately reflecting blood glucose control in all patients.
MIENTURNET is a web tool developed for microRNA-target enrichment and network-based analysis that prioritizes miRNA-target interactions through statistical analysis and network visualization.
SWIM identifies 171 switch genes that are all down-regulated in glioblastoma stem-like cells, including CAV1, COL5A1, COL6A3, FLNB, HMMR, ITGA3, ITGA5, MET, SDC1, THBS1, and VEGFC.

SWItchMiner (SWIM) is a software implementation designed to extract information from complex networks and identify hubs called “fight-club hubs.” Specifically, SWIM unearths “switch genes” characterized by unique connection patterns and clinical relevance. This tool can identify switch genes associated with changes in cell phenotype, and has been applied to cancer datasets and glioblastoma stem-like cells to discover potential biomarkers and therapeutic targets. SWIM is also amenable to detecting switch genes in various organisms and cell conditions.
A specific apolipoprotein E4 fragment, apoE4-165, promotes amyloid-peptide beta 42 (Aβ42) accumulation in human neuroblastoma SK-N-SH cells.
EpiMethylTag is a method developed for the simultaneous detection of ATAC-seq or ChIP-seq signals with DNA methylation.
KLF4 is involved in the organization and regulation of pluripotency-associated three-dimensional enhancer networks.
Agouti-related peptide is expressed in neurons along with neuropeptide Y and is indirectly inhibited by GLP-1 via GABA-dependent signaling in the arcuate nucleus.

Ketohexokinase-A acts as a protein kinase, phosphorylating and activating phosphoribosyl pyrophosphate synthetase 1 to promote de novo nucleic acid synthesis and hepatocellular carcinoma formation, and it also appears to play a distinct role in the development of diabetic kidney disease.

Yes, the major royal jelly proteins and yellow proteins of Drosophila form a protein family termed the MRJP/yellow family, and Yellow proteins are related to the Royal Jelly proteins. Additionally, the Drosophila genome encodes proteins that are highly identical to Yellow, and honey has a proteome resembling the royal jelly proteome.

SpectralTAD is an R package developed for defining a hierarchy of topologically associated domains using spectral clustering, and it outperforms other TAD callers in both simulated and experimental settings.
regutools is an R package available through Bioconductor that facilitates programmatic access to RegulonDB data for bacterial regulatory networks.

Yes, a draft assembly of the Apis mellifera filamentous virus genome and the Apis mellifera nuclear and mitochondrial genomes are available, and sequences can be mined to identify honey bee subspecies.

The genes implicated in nondystrophic myotonias include CLCN1 and SCN4A, which encode chloride and sodium channels respectively, as well as mutations in the CLCN1 gene such as c.2680C > T (p.Arg894*) and c.1238C>G causing p.Phe413Cys.
Yes, the olive pollen proteome has been studied, as available genomics data was combined with a comprehensive proteomics approach to get an annotated olive tree.

Yes, E-cadherin is a single-pass transmembrane protein that mediates cell-cell adhesion and is found along the length of contacting membranes of cells, indicating it resides within the plasma membrane. Additionally, IGF-II acts on an E-cadherin/beta-catenin complex at the membrane, and E-cadherin associates with beta-catenin at the membrane-distal region of its cytosolic domain. These findings demonstrate its presence as a plasma membrane marker.

A susceptibility locus on chromosome 13 and hypermethylation at imprinted loci like Dlk1-Dio3 impact the stability of genomic imprinting in mouse pluripotent stem cells, with demethylase stimulation by ascorbic acid preventing instability.
The shikimate pathway leads to the biosynthesis of aromatic amino acids and a range of natural products, including folate, chlorophylls, carotenoids, and anthocyanins.

Uber-operons are groups of operons that are evolutionarily or functionally associated through operons in other genomes. They represent footprints of operon evolution and can be identified by finding functionally or transcriptionally related operons with conserved gene sets across genomes. Prediction of uber-operons may lead to new tools for understanding biological pathways and networks, and a better understanding of genome structures and evolution. In *Escherichia coli* K12, 158 uber-operons covering 1830 genes have been predicted.

While the context details intellectual disability as a phenotype associated with 1p21.3 microdeletions involving DPYD and MIR137, it also indicates that deletions in the long arm of chromosome 1, overlapping a critical region, are associated with a phenotype consisting primarily of obesity, intellectual disability and autism-spectrum disorder, though a key gene specifically involved in the syndromic obesity phenotype is not explicitly identified.

Yes, cabergoline is used for treatment of Nelson’s syndrome. Several reports detail its use, demonstrating normalization of ACTH levels and even disappearance of pituitary tumors in patients with the syndrome, and it appears to be more effective than bromocriptine. Long-term treatment with cabergoline may be efficient, and it can induce remission of the syndrome.

Yes, mucins are glycosylated proteins because they are described as long glycoprotein molecules and have glycosylation sites present throughout their sequences, particularly within intrinsically disordered regions. Additionally, studies focus on N-glycosylation and the impact of glycosylation on mucin structure and function.

Yes, carpal tunnel syndrome is a common type of entrapment neuropathy resulting from pressure on a peripheral nerve as it passes through a narrow canal. It is specifically described as the most common nerve entrapment of the upper extremity and an entrapment of the median nerve in the wrist.

The main manifestation of Liebenberg syndrome is a transformation of the upper limbs to reflect lower limb characteristics, including dysplasia of the elbow, abnormal carpal bone shape, and brachydactyly.

Enasidenib and ivosidenib, IDH2 and IDH1 inhibitors respectively, developed by Agios Pharmaceuticals have been approved by the Food and Drug Administration for the treatment of adult relapsed or refractory acute myeloid leukemia with IDH2 and IDH1 mutations.

RADICL-seq is a technology that maps genome-wide RNA-chromatin interactions in intact nuclei. It is a proximity ligation-based methodology that reduces bias for nascent transcription and increases genomic coverage compared to existing methods. This technology identifies patterns of genome occupancy for different transcripts and cell type-specific RNA-chromatin interactions, highlighting transcription’s role in establishing chromatin structure.
Anti-Siglec-8 antibody is a potential treatment for eosinophilic gastritis, eosinophilic duodenitis, eosinophilic gastroenteritis, eosinophilic esophagitis, and hypereosinophilic syndromes.

Mammalian melatonin receptors are G protein-coupled receptors that regulate seasonal reproduction and circadian rhythms, with identified subtypes including MT1, MT2, and MT3. These receptors mediate effects such as inhibition of adenylyl cyclase and modulation of neuronal firing, prolactin secretion, and circadian rhythm phase shifts. Research indicates that MT1 and MT2 receptor activation inhibits cAMP signaling, influencing clock gene expression, while studies have not found significant mutations in known melatonin-related receptors associated with adolescent idiopathic scoliosis.

Holoprosencephaly is a brain abnormality resulting from the incomplete cleavage of the forebrain during early development, and it is the most common malformation of this region. It’s a heterogeneous disorder with a wide spectrum of severity, ranging from forms incompatible with life to subtle midline facial differences, and can be caused by chromosome aberrations, environmental factors, or mutations in over ten identified genes. This condition is characterized by a failure of the brain to separate into two hemispheres and can involve abnormalities in white matter, as well as various other brain structures.

In Drosophila neural progenitor cells, Numb regulates Notch receptor trafficking to late endosomes, controlling the balance between receptor recycling and degradation. Numb suppresses a recycled Notch receptor subpopulation, and excess Notch signaling in its absence requires Rab11 activity. This regulation by Numb influences signaling output after asymmetric cell division, impacting the differentiation process. Additionally, Numb and Notch form a regulatory feedback loop to control asymmetric cytokinesis and differentiation.

SAR425899 has been tested in randomized, placebo-controlled, double-blind clinical trials involving both healthy overweight volunteers and overweight/obese patients with type 2 diabetes. These trials evaluated the safety, pharmacokinetics, and pharmacodynamics of the drug. Additionally, it was tested in a 28-day study of overweight to obese subjects with type 2 diabetes to evaluate changes in insulin sensitivity and beta-cell function.

No. The provided information details a link between rare variants in PPARG and an increased risk of type 2 diabetes, specifically noting that carrying loss-of-function variants is associated with a substantial increase in risk of type 2 diabetes. There is no mention of any connection to type 1 diabetes.

Pexidartinib inhibits the Colony-Stimulating Factor (CSF1)/CSF1 receptor pathway, which reduces cell line proliferation and promotes autophosphorylation of the ligand-induced CSF1 receptor. As a selective CSF1 R inhibitor, it impacts the survival and proliferation of monocytes and the transition to a macrophage phenotype, ultimately affecting tumor growth and inflammation. This mechanism makes pexidartinib a potential systemic agent for treating Tenosynovial Giant Cell Tumor (TGCT).

Pore forming toxins include listeriolysin O, perfringolysin O, pneumolysin, pyolysin, streptolysin O, Panton-Valentine leukocidin, γ-haemolysin AB, CB, leukocidin ED, and those utilized by Staphylococcal leukocidins.
Kabuki syndrome is commonly caused by mutations in the histone-modifying enzyme lysine methyltransferase 2D (KMT2D).

Yes, steroid 5 alpha-reductase deficiency can lead to male pseudohermaphroditism, which is a form of intersex, and one case of true hermaphroditism was reported alongside a case of 5-alpha-reductase deficiency. The deficiency causes disturbances in sex differentiation and results in symptoms of male pseudohermaphroditism due to impaired dihydrotestosterone formation.

Yes, ubrogepant has entered clinical phase III trials. Several documents mention phase III clinical trials evaluating ubrogepant’s efficacy and safety as an abortive migraine treatment, including ACHIEVE I and ACHIEVE II trials demonstrating its effectiveness. Additionally, the context states that ubrogepant received FDA approval based on positive phase III outcomes.
The HOTAIR CNE is 32 nucleotides long.
The narcolepsy pentad includes excessive daytime sleepiness, cataplexy, sleep paralysis, hypnagogic or hypnopompic hallucinations, and disturbed nocturnal sleep.

Osteolectin is an osteogenic growth factor required to maintain adult skeletal bone mass that acts on the Leptin Receptor and functions as an endogenous inhibitor of Fibroblast Activation Protein to promote vertebrae mineralization.

Extracorporeal membrane oxygenation is a lifesaving therapeutic approach used in both adults and newborns suffering from cardiac or respiratory failure, functioning as a means of providing time for acutely damaged lungs to heal or supporting patients with reversible respiratory insufficiency.

The Yaa gene is associated with a lupus-like autoimmune syndrome, systemic lupus erythematosus (SLE), murine AIDS, and arthritis, and it accelerates autoimmune diseases in some autoimmune-prone strains of mice while also potentiating immune responses against self and foreign antigens.
Bimagrumab blocks activin type II receptors, preventing the activity of myostatin and other negative skeletal muscle regulators.

Yes, PDXK mutations cause axonal polyneuropathy, and are linked to the condition via reduced PDXK enzymatic activity and low pyridoxal 5'-phosphate levels. Affected individuals may become wheelchair-bound and blind if untreated, but clinical and biochemical profiles can be rescued with pyridoxal 5'-phosphate supplementation.

Yes, avelumab has been approved by the Food and Drug Administration for the therapy of urothelial carcinoma and is among recent novel therapeutic advances gaining approvals for treatment of advanced-stage disease. It is also approved for platinum-treated urothelial carcinoma and is being investigated for the management of urothelial malignancies.

Circadian proteomics studies have been performed in multiple organs and tissues, including the liver, heart, skeletal muscle, and hematologic malignancies such as diffuse large B-cell lymphoma and acute lymphocytic and myeloid leukemias.

Yes, Multisystem Inflammatory syndrome in children (MIS-C) is a rare but serious complication associated with COVID-19, initiated by an overactive immune response usually weeks after exposure to the virus. It is a dangerous pediatric complication of COVID-19 and a unique clinical complication of SARS-CoV-2 infection observed in pediatric patients.
RNA polymerase II transcribes enhancer RNAs from the domain of transcription enhancers.
The three phase 3, randomized, double-blind, placebo-controlled studies assessing galcanezumab were REGAIN, EVOLVE-1 and EVOLVE-2, and CONQUER.

No. While BRCA2 mutations are associated with increased cancer risk and targeted screening for prostate cancer in men with BRCA1 or BRCA2 mutations is investigated, there is no mention of a co-loss of BRCA2-RB1 or its association with prognosis in the provided information. One study suggests co-existence of BRAF V600E/BRCA1/2 may represent a unique subset of advanced colorectal cancer with a better prognosis, but this is not prostate cancer and does not involve RB1.

The ALPHEUS trial investigated ticagrelor and clopidogrel as treatments for patients undergoing percutaneous coronary intervention, with ticagrelor being compared to clopidogrel to reduce periprocedural myocardial necrosis.
Mast cells selectively and abundantly produce alpha/beta tryptases.

BCG immunotherapy is used to treat bladder cancer, including superficial transitional cell carcinomas, high-grade non-muscle invasive bladder cancer, and carcinoma in-situ, as well as malignant melanoma.
PRC1 catalyzes H2A monoubiquitination and, in some instances, is linked to histone modifications including trimethylation at lysines 9 and 27.

Yes, erenumab is a human CGRP-receptor antibody and inhibits calcitonin gene-related peptide-induced vasodilatory responses. It is also described as a monoclonal antibody against the calcitonin gene-related peptide receptor.
ARID1B is the key gene behind 6q microdeletion syndrome, impairing normal brain development and contributing to the associated phenotype.
Brensocatib was tested for the treatment of bronchiectasis, a disease characterized by frequent exacerbations potentially related to neutrophilic inflammation.
Cuf1 is a transcription factor involved in both copper acquisition and detoxification, regulating copper homeostasis and the expression of copper transport genes like *ctr4(+)*.
The Baltimore classification system defines seven groups of viruses based on their routes of replication and protein expression.

Dkk1 functions as an antagonist of Wnt signaling, preventing the formation of the Frizzled-Wnt-LRP6 complex and blocking Wnt/LRP6 signaling. It can stabilize LRP6 and prevent its down-regulation induced by Wnt ligands, though it doesn’t always promote LRP6 internalization or degradation. While necessary for proper neural development, overexpression of Dkk1 is linked to neurodegenerative diseases and can contribute to disease pathogenesis in multiple myeloma by inhibiting osteoblast differentiation.

Bimagrumab is a human monoclonal antibody that inhibits activin type II receptors (ActRII). It has anabolic action on skeletal muscle mass by blocking myostatin and other negative regulators of muscle growth, and is being evaluated for conditions like muscle wasting and obesity. Additionally, bimagrumab alters the function of pituitary gonadotroph cells and impacts pituitary-gonadal axes, though its effects on androgen secretion are minimal.
BOS172722 (CCT289346), CCT271850, NMS-P715, and CCT251455 are orally bioavailable MPS1 kinase inhibitors.

Yes, radiosurgery, specifically Gamma Knife radiosurgery (GKRS), can be used for Dysembryoplastic Neuroepithelial Tumors (DNET). It is mentioned as a treatment option in selected cases, and has been used successfully as a salvage treatment following subtotal resection or for recurrent components of these tumors, resulting in seizure control.

Commensal bacteria are microorganisms that compose the microbiota, found abundantly in mucosal organs like the intestine. These bacteria are essential for host physiological functions, including maintaining barrier homeostasis, regulating metabolism, and influencing inflammation and immune development. They can also modulate the host response to cancer immunotherapy and may act as pathobionts, becoming opportunistic pathogens under certain conditions.
Kabuki syndrome is a rare congenital disorder associated with pathogenic variants of both the KMT2D and KDM6A genes.
C/EBPbeta, STAT3, and Icsbp are transcription factors that regulate emergency granulopoiesis.
Eptinezumab received FDA approval in the USA in February 2020 for the preventive treatment of migraine in adults.

A web server at http://braineacv2.inf.um.es/ provides access to a resource of regulatory data, including information about splicing eQTLs enriched for regulatory information of neuron-specific genes in the human basal ganglia.

Yes, minocycline is a credible repurposed treatment candidate for mild Alzheimer disease and has been studied at dosages of 400 mg/d or 200 mg/d for 24 months to determine if it can modify cognitive and functional decline in patients with the condition.

Pyroptosis is an inflammatory form of cell death triggered by inflammasomes and mediated by gasdermin D. It results in the activation of cytokines and pore-mediated cell lysis, and was originally understood as caspase-1-mediated monocyte death in response to bacterial insults. Recent discoveries reveal pyroptosis is not cell type specific and can be triggered by pathogens like *Candida albicans*, ultimately influencing processes like immunity, disease, and even cancer.

A HapMap is a publicly available catalogue of common genetic variants in humans, currently including several million SNPs across 1115 individuals spanning 11 different populations, and is aimed at describing patterns of DNA sequence variation across the entire genome.

TWIST1 expression is associated with breast, liver, prostate, and gastric cancers, and patients with Saethre-Chotzen syndrome, caused by mutations in the TWIST1 gene, have an increased risk of developing breast cancer.

The RESILIENT study investigated depressive symptom trajectories in breast cancer patients and identified four latent classes: Resilient, Subsyndromal, Delayed, and Peak. Researchers found that younger, female, and non-white participants with higher anxiety were more likely to fall into certain classes. Variations in cytokine genes, age, and performance status predicted membership in the Resilient versus Subsyndromal classes, suggesting a genetic influence on depressive symptom variability.

The thrombotic thrombocytopenic purpura pentad includes thrombocytopenia, microangiopathic hemolytic anemia, neurological symptoms or abnormalities, fever, and renal dysfunction or abnormalities, though the pentad is often incomplete, especially in patients with HIV.

Organoids are three-dimensional structures that can be propagated in culture and represent a breakthrough in cell biology. They are constructed from self-organizing stem cells and can accurately replicate tumor heterogeneity and the microenvironment *in vitro*. These models are valuable for studying development, disease modeling, drug testing, and can even serve as a cell source for therapies or “living biobanks”.
Botulinum toxin has therapeutic uses for conditions including strabismus, blepharospasm, and numerous neuromuscular, autonomic, and sensory disorders.

Tcf3 functions as a cell-intrinsic inhibitor of pluripotency genes in embryonic stem cells, influencing the balance between pluripotency and differentiation. It is an integral component of the core regulatory circuitry of these cells and occupies promoters alongside pluripotency regulators like Oct4 and Nanog. Inhibiting Tcf3 increases stemness, cell proliferation, and master regulatory stemness genes, while its expression represses pluripotency genes such as Esrrb, Tfcp2l1, and Klf2. Ultimately, Tcf3 appears to counter pluripotency by competing with Sox2 at regulatory sites.
MK-1602, also known as ubrogepant, is used for the acute treatment of migraine and is also being studied for migraine prevention.
Yes, a missense variant in ADCY7 doubles the risk of ulcerative colitis, according to a whole-genome sequencing study.

Satralizumab is a humanized monoclonal antibody designed to treat neuromyelitis optica spectrum disorder by targeting the IL-6 receptor. It works by blocking B cell antibody production and has shown positive effects in reducing relapse risk for AQP4 seropositive patients. This treatment builds on experiences with tocilizumab, which also targets the IL-6 receptor, and was the third compound approved for NMOSD in the US market.

Yes, mucin overexpression is associated with disease. Aberrant hypermethylation can lead to mucin 13 overexpression in intrahepatic cholangiocarcinoma, contributing to metastasis. Additionally, MUC1 overexpression and hypoglycosylation are found in postoperative Crohn’s disease recurrence and correlate with recurrence severity.
The four major types of holoprosencephaly are alobar, semilobar, lobar, and middle interhemispheric variant, with additional classifications including types A and B lobar holoprosencephaly.
Five genes in *Saccharomyces cerevisiae* encode condensin components, including those encoding the core subunits smc2-8 and smc4-1, as well as genes encoding Cnd1, Cnd2, and Cnd3 in fission yeast.
The context does not contain information about a drug named AMG334.

Common epilepsies are considered to have a genetic basis, ranging from single-gene disorders to complex, non-mendelian traits. Genome-wide association studies have identified risk loci for epilepsy, including a region at 2q24.3 implicating SCN1A. Additionally, variations in genes like ME2, EFHC1, and CACNA1H have been linked to increased risk for idiopathic generalized epilepsies, though these represent non-mendelian inheritance patterns.

Yes, nerinetide may be effective for treating acute ischaemic stroke, particularly in patients undergoing rapid endovascular thrombectomy. It is a neuroprotectant effective in preclinical models and was assessed for efficacy and safety in a multicentre, double-blind, randomised controlled trial involving patients with large vessel occlusion within a 12-hour treatment window.

Alcohol acyl-transferases (AAT) synthesize volatile esters, which are a major class of compounds contributing to the aroma of many fruits. In melon, CM-AAT1 plays a major role in aroma volatile formation, producing a wide range of esters from combinations of alcohols and acyl-CoAs.  Specifically in apples, the AAT1 locus is critical for the biosynthesis of esters contributing to a ‘ripe apple’ flavor, and altering its expression impacts the fruit’s aroma.

Hikikomori is a severe form of social withdrawal syndrome impacting youths globally, initially recognized in Japan. It is characterized by social withdrawal lasting six months or longer, often centering lifestyle at home with no interest in school or work. This syndrome may be linked to modern societal changes, potentially serving as a coping strategy to avoid relationships and failure, and can be associated with conditions like thiamine deficiency and depression, even posing a risk for suicidal behaviors.
Mutations in the TWIST1 gene are primarily associated with Saethre-Chotzen syndrome, as it is implicated in both cytogenetic deletions and mutations that cause the syndrome.
Tofacitinib, baricitinib, filgotinib, and upadacitinib are four targeted synthetic DMARDs that function as Janus kinase inhibitors.

miR-301b is involved in targeting CYLD in triple-negative breast cancer, as it is upregulated in these cancers and directly downregulates CYLD at both mRNA and protein levels by interacting with its 3'-untranslated region.
The Candor trial tested the addition of daratumumab to carfilzomib and dexamethasone.
The provided texts do not contain information about the parts of a flagellum.

Male calico or tortoiseshell coat colors in cats are rare and associated with chromosome abnormalities, specifically an additional X chromosome. These cats often have an XXY-complement, a condition analogous to Klinefelter's syndrome in humans. While rare, some male tortoiseshell cats can be chimeras with a normal 38,XY/38,XY chromosome set, but this is uncommon.
Hepatocyte nuclear factor 4 alpha (HNF4) is a well-established master regulator of liver development and function.

Yes, ubrogepant, zavegepant, and atogepant are small-molecule calcitonin gene-related peptide (CGRP) receptor antagonists being developed for migraine treatment or prevention, and several others are in earlier stages of development. Olcegepant and telcagepant have also demonstrated clinical efficacy in treating migraine.
The putamen is the subcortical brain structure most influenced by common genetic variants, with a novel locus explaining 0.52% of the variance in its volume.
Givosiran is used for the treatment of acute hepatic porphyria and acute intermittent porphyria.

Mast cells release anaphylactic mediators into circulation, including tryptase, histamine, bradykinin, and various immunomodulatory compounds stored in secretory granules, and also heparin which activates the contact and coagulation systems.

Exencephaly is an uncommon malformation of the cranium involving a large, disorganized mass of cerebral tissue with absent flat bones of the calvaria, and can be a rare precursor to anencephaly or the most severe form of cranium bifidum.

Tumor necrosis factor-alpha (TNF) is overexpressed in obesity and is a candidate mediator of obesity-induced insulin resistance. It blocks insulin action and interferes with the insulin signaling cascade, impairing glucose transport. Evidence suggests TNF plays a role in modulating energy expenditure, fat deposition, and insulin resistance, and may be crucial in the link between obesity and diabetes. Reducing TNF-alpha function improves insulin sensitivity in obese mice.

No, eptinezumab is a humanized monoclonal antibody that binds to calcitonin gene-related peptide (CGRP) and blocks its binding to the receptor, while small molecule CGRP receptor antagonists are referred to as “gepants.”

The Enhancing NeuroImaging Genetics through Meta-Analysis (ENIGMA) Consortium is a global team science effort involving over 1400 scientists from more than 40 countries working to understand disease and genetic influences on the brain. It is a collaborative network that integrates neuroimaging and genetic data, analyzing information from tens of thousands of subjects across numerous institutions worldwide. ENIGMA addresses limitations of small sample sizes by combining data and employs standardized protocols, including meta- and mega-analyses, to increase statistical power and reproducibility of findings in neuroimaging studies. The consortium is organized into working groups focused on various neurological and psychiatric conditions, as well as methodological development for large-scale data analysis.
Inhaled Molgramostim can be used for treatment of autoimmune pulmonary alveolar proteinosis, a rare disease characterized by progressive surfactant accumulation and hypoxemia.

The main functions of the annexin family include roles in membrane organization and transport for epithelial polarity, association with ion channels as guiding or modulating proteins, acting as extracellular autocrine modulators of receptor function, binding to acidic phospholipids in a calcium-dependent manner, and potentially regulating cell proliferation and invasion through pathways like β-catenin/EMT, though some members may have calcium-independent functions and specialized tissue-specific roles.
The two vaccines for herpes zoster are Zostavax, a live attenuated vaccine, and Shingrix, a novel herpes zoster subunit vaccine.
Intronless genes are a characteristic feature of prokaryotes and may be inherited from the common ancestor of archaea, bacteria, and eukaryotes.
The development of ivosidenib is not explicitly stated, but it is indicated that it received approval from the FDA and is associated with Tibsovo.

Yes, apilimod is identified as one of 30 known drugs that inhibits viral replication and shows effective concentrations likely commensurate with therapeutic doses in patients. It is specifically mentioned as a PIKfyve kinase inhibitor.

No, lanabecestat did not slow cognitive or functional decline in patients with early Alzheimer’s disease or mild AD dementia. Both the AMARANTH and DAYBREAK-ALZ studies were terminated early after futility analysis, and there were no consistent, reproducible dose-related findings on primary or secondary efficacy measures.
The STING protein is activated by binding to cyclic dinucleotide ligands and through the catalytic synthesis of cyclic GMP-AMP by cyclic GMP-AMP synthase in response to cytosolic DNA.

Radio frequency ablation is a minimally invasive treatment option for both cancerous and non-cancerous tumors, including those in the kidneys, retroperitoneum, and pelvis. It utilizes thermal energy to destroy targeted tissue, and can be guided by laparoscopic, computerized tomography, or sonographic methods. While effective for small renal masses, particularly in infirm patients, recurrence rates may be higher with radio frequency ablation compared to cryoablation, and it carries a risk of complications like fistula or injury to the collecting system. It is also used to treat atrial arrhythmias by destroying arrhythmogenic myocardial zones.
The “28” in the Disease Activity Score 28 refers to the number of joints used in the scoring system to evaluate disease activity in rheumatoid arthritis.

The provided text does not contain information about EPICCURE. However, it details canSAR, a public integrative cancer-focused knowledgebase supporting translational research and drug discovery by integrating biological, pharmacological, chemical, structural biology, and protein network data. This knowledgebase aims to answer complex questions about proteins, cancers, and chemical tools used in experimental probing. Additionally, a study called CodeBreaK 100 investigated sotorasib therapy for advanced non-small-cell lung cancer patients with a specific mutation.

JASPAR 2020 expanded the CORE collection with 245 new profiles—169 for vertebrates, 42 for plants, 17 for nematodes, 10 for insects, and 7 for fungi—and updated 156 existing profiles, representing an 18% expansion overall, and included a novel collection of unvalidated TF-binding profiles with a community curation web form, a Q&A forum, updated genomic tracks, an inference tool, TF-binding profile similarity clusters, a RESTful API, and a JASPAR2020 R/Bioconductor package.

Yes, golimumab was tested in children and young adults with newly diagnosed type 1 diabetes to assess its impact on endogenous insulin production and exogenous insulin use. A phase 2 trial randomly assigned participants to receive golimumab or a placebo for 52 weeks.

Yes, G3BP1 is a component of stress granules and initiates their assembly by forming a multimer. It is also found occupying domains inside stress granules alongside other proteins and is essential for assembling them. Additionally, G3BP1 depletion impacts cellular responses to stress and cancer treatment, indicating its involvement in stress granule function.

Progeria is a rare and peculiar combination of dwarfism and premature aging, manifesting symptoms resembling aspects of aging at an early age. It can occur in both children (“child progeria” or Hutchinson-Gilford Progeria Syndrome) and adults (“adult progeria” or Werner’s syndrome), though these conditions do not entirely overlap with “normal” aging. While often considered an autosomal dominant disorder, there are cases suggesting a possible autosomal recessive inheritance, and it affects approximately one in several million births or one in 18 million individuals. It is caused by genetic mutations, including those affecting DNA repair or proteins within the nuclear envelope.

CrossICC is an R package designed for unsupervised clustering of gene expression data from multiple datasets and platforms. It does not require batch effect adjustment and utilizes an iterative strategy to determine the optimal gene signature and cluster numbers. The package also includes functions for visualizing identified subtypes and evaluating subtyping performance, with the goal of discovering robust cancer subtypes for personalized care. It is available on GitHub and Bioconductor under the GPL v3 License.
Finerenone blocks the mineralocorticoid receptor, acting as a nonsteroidal antagonist to this receptor.

Yes, colistin is an antibiotic. It is listed among 33 antibiotics active against UPEC strain UTI89 and is also mentioned in relation to synergistic activity with chloramphenicol against ESKAPE pathogens, and as a drug to which Acinetobacter baumannii can be resistant.

Yes, Tocilizumab is a humanized anti-interleukin-6 receptor (IL-6R) monoclonal antibody that competitively blocks IL-6 binding to the IL-6R and prevents the binding of IL-6 to membrane-bound and soluble IL-6 receptor. It is also described as an IL-6 antagonist.

No, DNA loop extrusion requires ATP hydrolysis. Human cohesin compacts DNA by loop extrusion and this compaction requires adenosine triphosphate. Additionally, cohesin extrusion is thought to play a central role in establishing genome architecture and requires cohesin ATPases, and condensin-mediated DNA compaction is ATP-enhanced. Even the reinitiation of DNA translocation by a motor protein requires ATP binding and extrusion of an initial DNA loop.
Rifampin, phenytoin, and carbamazepine are potent inducers of CYP3A4, likely resulting in a reduced hypnotic effect of zolpidem when used concomitantly.

Yes, mutational load considerations suggest the functional fraction within the human genome cannot exceed 25%, and is likely considerably lower. Additionally, there appears to be a possible upper limit of accuracy for functional annotation using co-evolution profiles.

Couvelaire uterus is a rare condition involving hematic infiltration of the uterine myometrium, typically due to a massive retroplacental hematoma. It is a nonfatal complication often linked to placental abruption, trauma, or factors affecting uterine-placental circulation. Diagnostically, it presents as dark purple or blue-purple discolorations of the uterine wall and can be identified through direct visualization or biopsy, though it may be underreported.
The OTOF gene encodes for otoferlin protein, which is expressed in the cochlear inner hair cells and is a major cause of nonsyndromic recessive auditory neuropathy spectrum disorder in humans.

Hypercapnia is a condition involving carbon dioxide retention, or an increase in CO₂ concentration in the blood. It is a known risk of diving and can cause mental and physical impairments, potentially leading to life-threatening accidents. Clinically, it is defined as an arterial blood partial pressure of CO₂ above 40 mmHg and is often seen in chronic lung disease. It can also suppress the HIF-dependent adaptive response to hypoxia.

TSA-Seq is a new mapping method that functions as a “cytological ruler” to estimate chromosomal distances from nuclear speckles genome-wide. It can predict chromosome trajectories between nuclear compartments without complex computational modeling. This method allows for genome-wide mapping of nuclear structure and provides insight into the spatial organization of transcription and gene expression.
The HercepTest uses a polyclonal antibody for immunohistochemical assessments of HER2 expression.

Participants in the 100,000 genomes project, including those with rare diseases or parents thereof, based their decisions to participate on trust in the NHS and a desire to improve future care. Involvement required additional travel and appointments, representing a clinical burden for those participating. Some participants expressed worries about the future of their relationships with healthcare professionals and the security of genomic data stored in biorepositories. The project also involved analyzing genomes from patients with cancer, sarcoma, and infection, with a pilot study including over 4600 participants from more than 2100 families.

Elagolix is an oral gonadotropin-releasing hormone receptor antagonist used to reduce heavy menstrual bleeding associated with uterine fibroids. It works by suppressing ovarian sex hormones and is often administered with add-back therapy, consisting of estradiol and norethindrone acetate, to manage hypoestrogenic effects. Clinical trials, including Elaris Uterine Fibroids 1 and 2, have demonstrated elagolix’s efficacy in reducing menstrual blood loss when combined with this hormonal add-back treatment. It is also undergoing development for this purpose both with and without low-dose hormone add-back therapy.
The organ of Corti is located along the length of the cochlear spiral within the inner ear’s ventral region.
